main_idea: You have a ship and an entire sea $to write your very own story...
entry_scene: The game is in development. No wipe release
region9: Strait of a Thousand Dead Man
TraderInSea_desc: Here you can replenish ammunition, find many trade offers, and even switch to PvP flags!
SmallVilage_desc: Here, you can replenish your ammunition, discover a few unique trade offers and even switch to PvP flags!
Town_desc: Here, you can replenish your ammunition or goods and build, buy, or sell a ship.
Altar_desc: Here, you can exchange Escudo coins for valuable items.
fr_change_activepberror: You cannot change factions during an active port battle or an attack window
not_available_blackmark: You cannot use this while under a black mark. Time left: {0} m.
not_available_quest: Not available due to an active quest
not_available_pirates: No available for pirates (except with peace pact)
auction_disable_pirates: Pirates can’t trade outside pirate cities
decraft_draft_tt1: You can disassemble blueprints into fragments
referal_bonus1: Reward for inviting a friend: Captain’s Seal +{0} days, +{1} battle marks
referal_bonus2: Referral reward received: +{0} ship slots, Captain’s Seal +{1} days to boost your progress
referal_bonus_many: You've received a special bonus for inviting {0} friends!
referal_rep_share: You've received {0} coins for topping up the accounts of invitees "{1}"
guildright_tt_AllowViewFortStorage: View and replenish the fort warehouse
guildright_tt_AllowEditPlayers: Editing ranks of members with lower rank
pb_notif_fort_mint_robbed: {0} gold was stored in the fort.
pb_notif_finishStep: Fort is now ready for final capture!
pb_notif_battlebegan: A marker has appeared on the World Map for entering battle.
achiv_1_desc: Sink the first ship in PvP
achiv_3_desc: Sink 3 other players' ships with durability below 20%
achiv_5_desc: Finish off another player's ship with a powder barrel
achiv_6_desc: Sink 2 ships with one shot from a mortar or round
achiv_7_desc: Escape pursuit with less than half remaining durability
achiv_12_desc: Win 5 consecutive matches in any mass mode on the Arena
achiv_13_desc: Win an Arena match with less than 30% durability and dealing over 400 damage
achiv_14_desc: Get the first place on the Arena as part of a team of three or more players. Counts as a victory.
achiv_15_desc: Win on the Arena in "Battle of Forts" mode without sinking once
achiv_17_desc: Win on the Arena in any mass mode, when your ship took no damage and you dealt 400+ damage
achiv_18_desc: Participate actively in a port battle, dealing over 2000 damage
achiv_19_desc: For sinking a Imperial Flagship with 20k+ durability alone (dealing more than 80% damage)
achiv_21_desc: Sink an NPS with higher durability than your own (but not less than 1000) without taking damage
achiv_22_desc: Complete a single-player mode with half or fewer guns on your ship
achiv_23_desc: For sinking a 1 rate ship on a 3-6 rate ship (dealing at least 20% damage)
achiv_24_desc: Win a boarding battle with a smaller crew
achiv_25_desc: Capture or plunder two consecutive merchant ships from the same squadron
achiv_26_desc: x2 experience for sinking an NPS with twice the durability of your ship
achiv_27_desc: Sink an enemy ship with a shot fired before your own ship is destroyed
achiv_28_desc: Awarded once for reaching 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30 captain ranks
achiv_29_desc: Smuggle without guns on board, without entering the port
achiv_30_desc: Successfully board three unique players in a row without entering a port
achiv_31_desc: x2 Battle Marks for sinking a player at sea with a higher-rated ship
achiv_32_desc: For raiding a fort belonging to a guild, player, or Imperial Flagship and stealing over 4000 cargo units
achiv_33_desc: Sink 5 player ships without entering the port or sinking
achiv_34_desc: Own more than 5 ships in the dock
achiv_35_desc: Own more than 10 ships in the dock
achiv_36_desc: Take the first place in an Arena tournament
achiv_37_desc: For an unfortunate death... within 30 seconds of leaving the port
achiv_38_desc: Complete 3 consecutive cargo missions in one game session
achiv_39_desc: Sink a player from the Legendary Board (PvP) with 3 or more points
achiv_40_desc: Collect 4 items at sea in less than a minute
achiv_41_desc: For every time you fill the experience bar after reaching rank 30
achiv_42_desc: Take the second place in the Arena tournament
achiv_43_desc: Enter port while having 5 million gold in your account
achiv_44_desc: Enter port while having 10 million gold in your account
achiv_45_desc: For reaching +100 reputation in any faction or -100 in all as part of a pirate guild.
achiv_46_desc: Trade in the Auction House with a total of 1 million gold in one game session
achiv_47_desc: Trade in the Auction House with a total of 10 million gold in one game session
achiv_48_desc: Increased reward for the top spot in the PB rankings (number of spots depends on participants)
achiv_49_desc: Open a total of 20 Specialty Workshops in total
achiv_50_desc: Contribute to the guild's victory in the tender and earn the Vizier status
achiv_51_desc: Complete a mode on epic difficulty with a 1-2-3 rate ship
achiv_52_desc: Collect 12 fully researched legends of the Age of Sail
achiv_53_desc: Open {0} chests using coins or keys
achiv_56_desc: Catch 5 whales in a row while at sea
achiv_57_desc: For destroying an Empire fort at sea with over 10,000 damage
achiv_58_desc: For winning bronze in the Arena Tournament
achiv_59_desc: For completing 5 daily quests in one day
cball_1_desc1: Standard all-purpose shot for dealing damage.
cball_2_desc1: Shells filled with flammable mixture. Deal #increased #damage by igniting ships or the water surface; great for triggering barrels. Fire works well against tough ships and clustered enemies.
cball_3_desc1: Two half-shot chained together to cause maximum #sail and rigging damage. Can #slow #enemy speed and manoeuvrability. Can destroy weapons on enemy's ship.
cball_4_desc1: Inflict high crew damage and have fast reload time. #Outnumbering enemies increases your odds in #boarding, and a strong advantage may let you take prisoners with fewer losses. Crew damage also slows #reload speed.
cball_12_desc1: Lightweight and #efficient shells that leave a burning mix when they hit water. Not recommended for frequent use due to their high cost.
cball_6_desc1: Designed for mortars. Deal area damage, with more impact closer to the center. Can damage multiple ships or land structures simultaneously.
cball_7_desc1: Costly rounds filled with white Bengal powder. They can #pierce #through the hull, damaging multiple ships in a row. Can be obtained during events, looted from Imperial Flagships, found at sea, or bought in the Shop.
cball_8_desc1: Designed for mortars. These shells deal area damage, more intense closer to the center. They have а limited range.
cball_5_desc1: Bombardment Shells charge encased in cast iron shells with fuses. Explode on impact, causing 10-20 area damage. Ship armor reduces its effectiveness. NPSs are particularly vulnerable to these bombs.
cball_s_desc1: Unique shots crafted using a mysterious technology. The finest projectiles one can fire from a cannon towards the enemy. Have a slower reload time
cball_13_desc1: Ammunition for #swivel guns is known for its long range and cost-effectiveness. Due to their small size, they inflict minor damage.
cball_14_desc1: Ammunition for #swivel guns that burst upon hitting a ship, enabling fire over obstacles or on specific trajectories marked by impact.
cball_16_desc1: Ammunition for #swivel guns. Are effective at long range, capable of area damage, and deal increased damage to sails.
cball_18_desc1: Ammunition for #swivel guns. Unique rockets with colorful fireworks! Available only during holidays.
pkeg_1_desc: A set of 4 barrels. They have a small blast radius and seem harmless, but together they create a large damage area.
pkeg_2_desc: A set of 2 barrels. After exploding, they leave a smoke screen and a burning resin spot on the water. Great for escaping pursuit.
pkeg_3_desc: A set of 3 barrels. Explosions slow enemy ships.
pkeg_4_desc: One large barrel with a wide blast radius and damage. When exploded not in water (e.g., on an enemy ship), it deals only 50% damage.
pkeg_5_desc: A mine hidden underwater and invisible to enemies. It has a huge blast radius and creates a breach in the enemy ship's hull upon detonation.
pkeg_6_desc: Mines hidden underwater and invisible to enemies. Dropping 4 mines simultaneously covers a large area. It slows down ships upon detonation.
pkeg_7_desc: Send a boat with a gunpowder barrow charge toward the targeted enemy! 3 sailors and 3 boarders will be sacrificed. You will see a highlight when the launch is possible.
res_1_desc: A basic material for shipbuilding and rigging. Lets you patch the hull at sea. Found everywhere and one of the main resources in ports. Can also be obtained from #sawmills or by #dismantling captured ships. Could the whole Archipelago be deforested...?
res_2_desc: Rum can be used to dilute water and extend its storage - or simply to lift the mood... That's why sailors ain't too fond of strict discipline, get it? Often paid instead of wages, for example to craftsmen when building cannons.
res_3_desc: Mostly made from hemp or flax. Best source is capturing and #breaking down ships for sailcloth, or spinning wool from #animals into fabric.
res_4_desc: Foundation for construction and casting, especially weaponry. Obtained by processing iron ore from #mines or #smelting old cannons in the workshop. Merchant ships often carry iron.
res_5_desc: Crystal-clear freshwater (for now) from local glaciers. Seawater, of course, isn’t good for drinking or cooking... Cities rely on #water supply managed by the port-owning faction. Without it, cities face shortages. Water can be freely traded at the auction or smuggled to certain bays.
res_6_desc: A food commodity with demand varying by region. Can be #sold in settlements and to sea traders. Sugar is also used for rum production.
res_7_desc: A drink that's quickly gaining popularity, though not as favored as sailors' rum yet. Beer brine helps preserve things, making it valuable to settlements and sea merchants.
res_8_desc: In the Archipelago, the slave trade is prohibited. Yet, it flourishes in pirate ports, where captives work in #mines and #factories, serving as servants on #personal islands.
res_9_desc: Fruits, tobacco, coffee, and spices are highly prized across the Archipelago and are vital ingredients in #provisions. They can be discovered while #exploring #islands
res_10_desc: In demand on #farms and settlements due to its relative cheapness. Often available from sea merchants. Nine happy rats in the hold out of ten!
res_11_desc: Crucial for alloys and #weapon production. Only gained by processing copper ore from #mines or #melting down old weapons in the workshop.
res_12_desc: Irreplaceable for iron forging, cannon casting, and heating northern settlements. Found in #coal mines or made by #burning wrecks in the workshop. Very expensive in cold regions; in the south, kids use it instead of snowballs.
res_13_desc: Pitch coating protects parts from moisture and makes the workers sticky. Produced at special #productions.
res_14_desc: Durable alloy made in the #workshop for high-quality #weapons and #upgrades. Bronze ingots are heavy enough to be parts or melee weapons.
res_15_desc: This is what the imperial currency is called in the Archipelago. Only these gold coins are accepted when building Imperial ships. They can also be traded for valuables at #altars of the “sea gods,” where local natives will gladly make a deal.
res_16_desc: A large iron part. Produced in the #workshop. Required for building large #ships and #fortifications.
res_17_desc: Thick, heavy fabric resistant to moisture, made in the #workshop. A strategic resource used, oddly enough, for sail making and repair.
res_18_desc: Coating parts with resin protects them from moisture and makes the ones who applied it sticky. Produced in specialized facilities at sea.
res_20_desc: What's left floating after a ship is destroyed. Can be burned into #coal in the workshop.
res_21_desc: Unlike rotten meat, this can be used for cooking. Part of #rations and hearty meals.
res_22_desc: Transporting animals is profitable but requires preparation. You'll also need to clean the hold and deck... The animals survive long voyages and provide #meat and #wool for clothing.
res_23_desc: Scattered across the #islands like an echo of the primeval volcanic eruption. Can be refined into iron or used in #cannon casting. Barrels with volcanic ore in the alloy are unmatched in quality
res_24_desc: An excellent way to store your #savings safely. To get the gold out… just open the chest in port. Even a small gold chest makes a great piece of decor.
res_25_desc: A unique wax-like substance highly valued by perfumers and medics of the Khanfar Caliphate. The only commodity that can be traded at auction during the #Tender. Disappears from all warehouses after the Tender ends!
res_27_desc: Made in the #workshop through woodcrafting. Versatile and used in building #ships and creating their #upgrades.
res_29_desc: Large iron part made in the workshop for #ship construction. You better secure it properly during transport, especially in storms, to not send your crew flying to Davy Jones' locker...
res_30_desc: Captain's silverware and pretty accessories. May be a bit rusty, but still valuable to some collectors or for #selling to #traders. Can be #melted in port to get copper.
res_31_desc: Mined from iron #mines. Smelted into #iron in the workshop.
res_32_desc: Mined from copper #mines. Can be turned into #copper at the workshop.
res_33_desc: This fancy-looking piece of paper lets you build any top-level mine or manufactory for free or upgrade the port warehouse to the fifth level.
res_34_desc: Freshly caught and cooked fish is a delicacy for sailors with a poor #diet. Often sold in ports, but best exchanged with special traders for rare goods.
res_36_desc: Can be crafted in the #workshop or “borrowed” from fishermen. If tackle is in your hold, fishing spots will be marked on the map. Tackle significantly #boosts your #catch and allows harpooning whales near your ship. There's more fish and whales up north.
res_37_desc: This currency lets you buy #rare #items from merchants and get approval from the admiralty for building certain ships or setting up new production. You can earn it through Arena and sea battles. Beware that trading it at an auction comes with extra taxes.
res_38_desc: An unusual find that merchants can exchange for #rare #items and upgrades. Whether it's a real skull or not, one can only guess.
res_39_desc: Food supplies for sailors and workers on the land, serving far from cities.
res_40_desc: This document lets you #upgrade a #fort to the highest level, instantly repair all structures, or build a specific ship.
res_42_desc: Use this blueprint to let the shipyard masters modify an existing upgrade or install a new one, including rare ones.
res_43_desc: Mysterious key. Collect them to open a chest and get rewards.
res_44_desc: Some ships can only be built using unique Imperial blueprints. You can find blueprint fragments by sinking Imperial flagships, and once you’ve studied enough, you can build a unique ship.
res_45_desc: Collect #fragments and visit a ragpicker to assemble a complete blueprint. The ragpicker can be found in ports (Workshop section) and charge a hefty fee for their services!
res_49_desc: A spice valued as highly as gold. It's also used as a dye for the clothing of the Archipelago's aristocrats.
res_50_desc: A sweet and mildly spicy seasoning made from special peppers. Used in many popular dishes. For example, the famous Laguno-Blanco omelet wouldn't be the same without paprika.
res_51_desc: Used as a fragrant spice and flavor enhancer.
res_52_desc: Essential everywhere, even though it's plentiful on the Archipelago. Salted meat can last at "room" temperature and is crucial for long voyages.
res_53_desc: Valued for its exquisite qualities and beauty. Only the wealthiest of the Archipelago can afford it. Trading silk is a path to riches.
res_54_desc: It's a treasure you can't walk on!
res_55_desc: Edible fruits from certain types of palm trees, usually eaten dried.
res_57_desc: Despite Bridgeport's medical warnings about the dangers of smoking pipes, tobacco's popularity and trade volume keep rising.
res_58_desc: Beans for brewing the most popular hot drink. Coffee's so essential, it keeps you from falling off the mast and breaking your neck in the middle of the night! It's not grown on the Archipelago, so we might run out one day...
res_60_desc: Goes great with almost anything, be it meat, poultry, or vegetables. Even if the cook's skills are as rough as the high seas, a pinch of pepper can save the day.
res_61_desc: A sweet spice, highly prized for the laborious process of cultivating vanilla pods.
res_63_desc: A sophisticated alcoholic drink. Winemaking and wine drinking are arts in themselves, much like the trade of fine wine.
res_64_desc: Fresh fruit on board is a rarity on long voyages. Highly prized, they help prevent scurvy and other diseases from lack of vitamins.
res_65_desc: Essential for cooking, making incense, and producing medicines.
res_66_desc: Watered-down rum with lime and cinnamon. Lasts longer than plain water, prevents illness, and brightens a sailor's hard life.
res_69_desc: These remarkable little fish, shaped like a chess knight, are in high demand among overseas merchants. Some say it’s due to a fashion among Khanfar nobles to keep them in aquariums... or perhaps it’s tied to the coming Year of the Horse.
res_70_desc: The main fuel for lamps and #lanterns, lighting up the darkest corners of the Archipelago. Ships carrying it can easily be found by their smell.
res_71_desc: Collect all parts of the mysterious map to reveal a #marker (yellow cross) on the map where #treasure is hidden.
res_72_desc: A special currency for "insiders." Only it can be used to get certain services from pirates... Can be found by sinking pirates and Order ships. Pirate bays have a trader willing to exchange tokens for rare items
res_73_desc: Collect all map parts to reveal a #marker (yellow cross) showing where #supplies are hidden. Pieces are found by sinking trade ships.
res_74_desc: Collect all map pieces to reveal a #marker (yellow cross) showing where the #treasure is hidden. Map fragments are found by sinking elite ships with a star.
res_75_desc: Collect all map parts to reveal a #marker (yellow cross) showing the location of a #camp. Pieces are found by sinking fanatic ships.
trading_item_generic_tt: These goods are not used for crafting, they can be sold to ports.
design_404_name: Ice Lantern (+5% speed, hold, and durability)
upgrade_2_tt: This universal upgrade enhances items that restore both temporary and permanent hull integrity.
upgrade_3_tt: Enhances durability - a universal upgrade for any ship.
upgrade_4_tt: Ideal for long-range engagements, particularly effective against armored vessels.
upgrade_5_tt: If the enemy has fire protection, the color of reduced durability above the ship turns gray.
upgrade_6_tt: If the enemy has bow protection, the color of reduced durability above the ship turns gray. Ramming is an effective way to quickly inflict damage, 'trading off' durability.
upgrade_8_tt: Quick ammo switching or a consumable item activated earlier can decide the battle's outcome.
upgrade_9_tt: Trade-off maneuverability for increased defense and boarding effectiveness.
upgrade_10_tt: A universal, but not always necessary upgrade. Best suited for chasing down an enemy.
upgrade_11_tt: Allows early detection of enemy ships.
upgrade_13_tt: The more swivel guns there are on a ship, the more effective this upgrade is.
upgrade_14_tt: The bonus gives mortars a 25-degree turn in either direction.
upgrade_15_tt: The enemy will see a "?" instead of the ship's crew count, so they cannot predict the outcome of a boarding fight.
upgrade_16_tt: Properly chosen maneuverability will allow you to realize the full potential of guns in both broadside and close combat.
upgrade_18_tt: High potential, though complex to utilize effectively; does not apply to mortars.
upgrade_21_tt: A long-range combat enhancement; does not apply to mortars.
upgrade_22_tt: If the enemy has fire protection, the color of reduced durability above the ship turns gray.
upgrade_23_tt: Sacrifice maneuverability to boost other characteristics, which can be justified by strategy.
upgrade_24_tt: Allows safer navigation in storms, including in the Endless Sea. Increases the chance to escape pursuit.
upgrade_25_tt: Most effective in mid and close-range battles, especially on ships with low maneuverability that often fail to turn for a broadside.
upgrade_26_tt: Offers unique protection against mortars, invaluable during Port Battles.
upgrade_28_tt: The effect of reducing losses does not stack with flags but works in addition.
upgrade_29_tt: Losing maneuver will make it harder to aim mortars at targets and retreat, but the increase in range can be justified.
upgrade_30_tt: You can loot gold from the enemy during boarding and store it in chests, but if you lose, the enemy can take your gold.
upgrade_31_tt: Increase the speed bonus. The ship gradually gains additional speed by moving in cruising mode (under full sails)
upgrade_32_tt: Increase the speed bonus. The ship gradually gains additional speed by moving in cruising mode (under full sails)
upgrade_33_tt: Increase the speed bonus. The ship gradually gains additional speed by moving in cruising mode (under full sails)
upgrade_34_tt: Increase the speed bonus. The ship gradually gains additional speed by moving in cruising mode (under full sails)
upgrade_35_tt: Speed increases when inspecting anything but does not affect crew landing.
upgrade_36_tt: Significantly increases the firing angle of mortars.
upgrade_37_tt: Does not apply to fire ships
unit_sailor_1_text: Sailors are needed to operate the ship. If there are not enough sailors, #reloading and #maneuvering will be slower. Extra sailors do not increase #speed, but provide a crew reserve.
unit_musketeer_1_text: Participates in #boarding actions, providing long-range fire support.
unit_boarding_2_text: Participates in #boarding actions, known for their enhanced resilience.
map_passing1_d: A small military patrol, the only hope against a pirate raid on a remote trade route, barely manages its combat mission. Defend the law and destroy all enemies.
map_passing2_d: You are tasked with transporting the new governor to Bridgetown. Midway, you fall into an ambush. Fulfill your duty to the end: protect the governor and crush the enemy.
map_passing3_d: The Order of the New Ark invites you to join the ranks of the 'chosen.' To earn the title of a knight in the Order, you must endure the crucible of combat against numerous foes. The mission requires more than mere survival; you must also protect as many of your fellow Order ships as possible - your future comrades in arms.
map_passing4_d: Beyond the howling wind and the noise of waves crashing against icebergs, unknown ships guard burials near the glacier. According to one legend, colorful islands existed here before the Cataclysm. The first ice cutter settlements reportedly uncovered a dreadful mystery, after which they were destroyed and buried.
map_passing6_d: The Imperial fleet conducts a traditional battle on the Blood Moon. Young lieutenants face their trial by combat. Achieve victory over the "blacks" and disrupt their examination.
map_passing5_d: The most renowned and heavily guarded route in the Endless Sea leads directly to buried treasures. Daily, various captains try to lead their crew to the end, but few return...
map_passing9_d: After the Cataclysm, a certain group of people renounced society and established a settlement. Their intentions are far from noble - they consider all common people unworthy and, reportedly, are preparing a series of attacks in the Archipelago.
map_passing10_d: According to found records, the "Order of the New Ark" received technology from the empire that could create a ship capable of navigating through the storms of the Endless Sea. Whether this is true remains to be discovered...
map_passing11_d: The leader of the "black pirates" vanished after the reconstruction of Tortuga, along with his entire personal fleet, which was enhanced with the latest technologies. Whose side he is on now is unknown, but coordinates are available for those who wish to search for him.
map_passing12_d: You found a map that leads around an iceberg to its top. Although you found no burials here, smoke is visible ahead...
pmd_ImprovedNpcCrewAndReload: Enemies have more crew and reload faster
pmd_ImprovedNpcsAmmo: Improved weaponry and damage of enemy ships
pmd_PowderKegsEverywhere: Gunpowder barrels and mines are everywhere
pmd_PlayerCurse: Curse: your durability decreases over time
pmd_StrongWind: Strong wind: sails are damaged at the 3rd speed
pmd_Towers: Fortifications: additional structures will attack you
select_character: Select the start of the story:
tt_PortTopWeapons: With this workshop, you can craft the finest Weapons and manufacture ammunition in a separate section of the workshop, ("Miscellaneous" tab). You can equip and dismantle this workshop like any other.
skill_1_desc: Ability to post 5 lots of goods and orders on the exchange
skill_2_desc: Post up to 20 lots of goods and orders on the exchange
skill_3_desc: Create more items at the port with a limit of 600 craft-time units and a respawn rate of 40 units per hour
skill_4_desc: Increases the craft-time limit to 1000 units and the respawn rate to 50 units per hour
skill_5_desc: Enter ports through the lighthouse under any flag except military (only if port entry is allowed)
skill_6_desc: Store more resources across the world by having up to 6 warehouses
skill_7_desc: Store even more resources: up to 11 warehouses
skill_8_desc: Fast travel twice in a row
skill_11_desc: Unlocks contraband quests. Good profit, but peace flags are unavailable
skill_12_desc: Purchase ammunition at cost from merchants and settlements at sea and encounter more trading opportunities.
skill_13_desc: Build up to 4 different mines and manufactories, instead of 2
skill_14_desc: Allows the construction of 6 different mines and manufactories.
skill_15_desc: High-level mines and manufactories will keep running without provisions, but their production rate will slow down to just below level 2. This doesn't affect farms.
skill_16_desc: Cruising speed is higher when sailing on a broad reach with the wind (the wind direction indicator turns green).
skill_17_desc: Reduces the aggression distance of dangerous NPSs by a third
skill_18_desc: Enhance your view with the spyglass to zoom in on objects and reveal more information
skill_19_desc: Cut mooring fees by 50% (does not affect Peace Flags)
skill_20_desc: Gain a +5 unit bonus to the craft-time limit for each spec. workshop across different ports
skill_21_desc: You will be able to see the resources in players' holds when hovering over a target, just like with NPSs.
skill_23_desc: You can take up to 4 quests from the tavern
skill_22_desc: Some durability is restored following a successful boarding
skill_24_desc: Take up to 3 ships into your squadron by boardings or bribing
skill_25_desc: Captured ships will be repaired by 50% of their durability.
skill_27_desc: Set a 2-4 minute fuse on powder barrels. For mines +1 minute
skill_28_desc: Hire more spec. crew on your ship: 1+ place
skill_31_desc: Single cannon shots become 50% more accurate and 50% more effective at penetrating the stern and bow of an enemy ship.
skill_32_desc: Firefighting is now an automatic background task performed by your crew, no shouting necessary
skill_33_desc: Increase the warehouse capacity of all mines and manufactories by 25%.
skill_34_desc: Execute a quick and concentrated broadside salvo that can also detonate sinking ships. Switch with [Alt]. A 3-second delay follows each salvo
skill_36_desc: Increase the operational speed of mines and manufactories in dangerous waters by 25%
skill_37_desc: Production of beams and bulkheads is 7% cheaper in ports
skill_38_desc: Reduce the cost of producing plates and bronze in ports by 10%
skill_39_desc: Casting the best weapons in ports with a weapons workshop is 10% cheaper
skill_47_desc: You will be able to travel twice in a row instead of once between neighboring non-pirate ports, without resources.
skill_48_desc: Damage to the crew from any ammo type, except for grapeshot, will be increased by a third
skill_49_desc: The сonsumable item limit in the warehouse is increased to 400
skill_50_desc: Build 2 Specialty Workshops in dangerous water ports without warehouse ties.
skill_51_desc: Dismantling captured ships yields 33% more wood and fabric, plus a few beams
skill_52_desc: Fire salvoes from both sides simultaneously by holding [Alt] and the fire button
skill_53_desc: You can own a second personal island and travel between them.
skill_56_desc: Special crew wages are reduced by a third
skill_57_desc: Captured ships will receive less damage when losing command
skill_58_desc: Take prisoners in addition to the captured crew during boardings. We can always find use for them...
skill_59_desc: Three personal islands are even better than two. The travel interval is reduced, allowing for faster movement between islands.
CaptainSkillsInfoWindow_20: Only one of the two skill groups can be developed
skill_cantberesetted_1: Cannot reset this skill while building slots are in use
skill_cantberesetted_3: Cannot reset this skill while island slots are in use
skill_cantberesetted_5: Cannot reset this skill while warehouse slots are in use
skill_cantberesetted_4: Cannot reset this skill while you exceed the limit of certain consumable items {0}
skill_cantberesetted_2: To reset this skill, you must first reset the dependents
skill_cantberesetted_6: Cannot reset skill while slots for captured ships are in use
skill_cantberesetted_7: Cannot reset this skill while there is a second workshop in {0}
skill_cantberesetted_Q: After resetting, skill points (if available) will be refunded, but gold will not be returned. Reset this skill?
ship_2_desc: A classic topsail schooner with light armament. It became famous for delivering the news of Nelson's victory at the Battle of Trafalgar to England. Why is it that new captains arrive in the Archipelago aboard this schooner?...
ship_3_desc: "Red Seal Ship". Such ships were used by the Tokugawa shogunate for trade and diplomatic missions to Southeast Asian ports. Upon reaching the Archipelago, it was converted into an excellent warship.
ship_4_desc: Built in 1752 in Toulon, she was successfully used to bombard Berber pirate bases in Algeria.
ship_5_desc: The Venetian fleet kept armed polaccas in service to protect trade routes in the Adriatic Sea from Dalmatian, Greek, and Turkish pirates
ship_6_desc: The brigantine gained fame among pirates thanks to its seafaring capabilities, maneuverability, and speed. Even the name changed its meaning, literally becoming "brigantine" (in Italian, brigante means "outlaw, pirate")
ship_7_desc: First built by Maurice "Shipwright" Fleury in the port of Aruba. After falling victim to a plot by the Levenhook brothers, Maurice was tragically executed. In his memory, a skeleton holding a chalice - a symbol of grief - and a spear - a symbol of revenge - adorns the bow.
ship_8_desc: The first two of these ships were spotted near Grey Island, crewed by Russian sailors and officers who fled the Tsar's rule in search of freedom.
ship_9_desc: These clumsy but reliable ships sailed between the New and Old Worlds for over 200 years, transporting goods and treasures, including gold from South America to Spain. They became prime targets for pirates, leading to a long conflict between brigantines and galleons.
ship_10_desc: A sail frigate of the Russian Empire, laid down in 1724 during the reign of Peter the Great by master shipbuilder Richard Browne. Launched in 1728, it participated in two wars, and in 1734, during the blockade of Danzig, it captured the French 30-gun frigate "Le Brilliant".
ship_11_desc: A heavily-armed American frigate. It served in the First Barbary War and the War of 1812, where it captured 13 whaling ships during a single cruise. Later captured by the British, the "Essex" went on to serve under the British Crown.
ship_12_desc: The combat galleon of the pirate prince Morgan Black, rumored to have been gifted to him by the Empire for his service. It's one of the best-armed ships of its class.
ship_13_desc: Tartanes were widely used across the Mediterranean - from Turkey to Spain - for a variety of tasks. They were especially favored by Algerian corsairs. Upon reaching the Archipelago, the tartane became popular with local pirates, particularly after Tortuga craftsmen fitted it with mortars.
ship_14_desc: The "Devourer" is an Imperial ship with heavy armament on both the bow and stern. Its thick hull offers excellent protection, while jibs ensure good maneuverability.
ship_15_desc: A revolutionary Dutch ship from the late 16th century, designed to optimize transoceanic voyages by maximizing hold capacity and crew efficiency. The fluyt gave Dutch traders a competitive edge, helping the Netherlands rise to naval power.
ship_16_desc: Made from high-quality lightweight woods, this reconfigured combat schooner boasts excellent seafaring abilities and maneuverability. Pirates once mistook its flashy appearance for a sign of an inexperienced traveler - only to fall into an ambush themselves.
ship_17_desc: Originally designed for trade expeditions and provisioning cities with its massive hold, this ship can also hold its own in combat. It gained an unfortunate reputation as a slave transporter during the height of the Atlantic slave trade.
ship_18_desc: Originally a Dutch fluyt, it underwent significant redesign after arriving at the Archipelago. Many mistake it for the lightly armed "Friede", only to realize their error too late as mortar shells tear through their decks.
ship_19_desc: A warship with an impressive service record. She fought at the Battle of the Saintes on April 9, 1782, under Admiral Sir George Rodney against Admiral de Grasse. In 1794, she was rebuilt as a large 44-gun frigate and later took part in the Revolutionary and Napoleonic wars.
ship_20_desc: A 17th-century East Indiaman, belonging to the Dutch East India Company and the largest ship in their fleet. Her maiden voyage to the Dutch East Indies was in 1651. After several voyages, she sank near Madagascar in 1662. Fortunately, her blueprints are still in the hands of local shipwrights.
ship_22_desc: The "Black Wind" has a sturdy hull and can accommodate a large crew, providing solid protection in battle. However, this comes at the cost of a reduced cargo hold, making her less suitable for trade or prolonged engagements.
ship_23_desc: A Korean ship with a distinctive armored deck resembling a turtle shell, hence its name "turtle ship." The first of these ships fought the Japanese fleet at Sacheon. Local shipwrights further refined the design.
ship_24_desc: A lightweight and fast corvette of the French Navy with formidable armament. It took part in the First Franco-Mexican War and the bombardment of Fort San Juan de Ulua near Veracruz.
ship_25_desc: One of the original six frigates of the United States Navy, the "Constitution" was larger and more heavily armed than its contemporaries. She gained fame during the War of 1812 against Great Britain, earning the nickname "Old Ironsides" and the adoration of many.
ship_26_desc: Main combat ship of the Kai fleet. Has a fearsome appearance, sturdy hull, hybrid armament and mortars, allowing it to fight in any conditions
ship_27_desc: Launched on March 2, 1779, in Saint-Malo, the "Le Cerf" patrolled the coast of Brittany and fought its first battle against two English cutters on June 21. She became famous for her evasiveness.
ship_28_desc: With a fearsome appearance, powerful armament, a strong hull, and a large cargo hold, the "Firestorm" became a legendary Imperial ship. The first "Firestorms" were captured and studied after Longhorn's victory over the Immortal Admirals.
ship_31_desc: The flagship of Horatio Nelson, one of the greatest and largest warships of the British Royal Navy, immortalized at the Battle of Trafalgar. Launched in 1765, she remains the oldest commissioned warship in the world with 245 years of service as of 2023.
ship_32_desc: A French ship of the line from the late 17th century. Launched in 1691, she participated in the War of the League of Augsburg. During the War of Spanish Succession (1701-1713), she was captured by the English and destroyed in Vigo Bay in 1702.
ship_34_desc: Clippers are vessels with the most powerful sail rig and a unique hull design that minimized water resistance at the cost of maneuverability. This ship held the record for the fastest passage between New York and San Francisco - 89 days and 8 hours.
ship_35_desc: These Imperial ships were first deployed at the Battle of Devios as part of the "Iron Squadron." Slow, heavily armored, and equipped with powerful weaponry, they withstood the brunt of the Espanyola fleet. The battle site became known as Lagrimas de Viudas - Widows' Tears Sea.
ship_36_desc: The largest Spanish ship of the line, which fought at the Battle of Trafalgar. While heavy and unwieldy, she could easily devastate both land fortifications and entire squadrons of enemy frigates.
ship_38_desc: A distinguished ship-of-the-line of the Black Sea Fleet that has been through many battles. From 1784 to 1788, her captain was Fyodor Ushakov, the future legendary Russian admiral.
ship_39_desc: Originally a 17th-century Spanish Manila galleon, heavily armed and used to transport Spanish treasures from the Philippines to Mexico. After arriving in the Archipelago, the ship was redesigned and earned widespread fame.
ship_40_desc: The "turtle ship" served as the core of the Korean fleet during the 16th and 17th centuries. In addition to her unique covered design, she wields combined armament, including mortars.
ship_41_desc: Originally a French ship of the line, she was captured by Englishmen during the "Glorious First of June" battle in 1794. She served actively in the Royal Navy until 1807, participating in several engagements and capturing enemy vessels. In 1807, she was converted into a prison ship.
ship_43_desc: One of the fastest and best-armed ships in its class, making her a formidable opponent for medium frigates.
ship_44_desc: The first ship of the line approved after Lord Anson became First Lord of the Admiralty. She took part in the Seven Years' War, the American War of Independence, and the Napoleonic Wars.
ship_46_desc: A British ship of the line which served as inspiration for many of the largest warships during the 17th century. Considered to be the most powerful ship during its time, she was heavily armed but suffered from poor stability due to its weight.
ship_47_desc: French frigates and combat sloops were ineffective against the Berber pirates, who used the agile xebecs. Logically speaking, if they are able to make such good use of them, then why not try those xebecs ourselves? The original name, meaning "Shark" when translated from French, was launched in the year 1751 in Toulon, where it soon made the lives of those pirates more difficult.
ship_49_desc: The first ship of the line in the French Navy, "La Couronne" was laid down at the La Roche Bernard shipyard in 1629 at the personal initiative of Cardinal Richelieu. It served as the flagship during the Battle of Guetaria, where the new French fleet defeated a Spanish squadron.
ship_50_desc: A warship of the Baltic Fleet of the Russian Empire, designed by Peter the Great. It was the first of its class and one of the Tsar's favorite ships. During the Great Northern War, it served as the flagship of both the Russian and the combined Russo-Danish-Dutch-English fleets, occasionally sailing under Peter's personal standard.
ship_52_desc: A famed French ship of the line that duelled with HMS Victory during the Battle of Trafalgar. The fatal shot killing Vice-Admiral Horatio Nelson was fired from this very ship.
ship_53_desc: Constructed in 1681 with private funds by Charles Mordaunt, Earl of Peterborough, and named after him. The ship was too heavily armed for a private vessel, leading to its confiscation in 1683. After legal battles, it was later purchased by the Royal Navy.
ship_54_desc: The oared frigate of William Kidd, the pirate hunter who became a pirate himself. First launched in 1695, it sailed thousands of miles across the Atlantic and Indian Oceans.
ship_55_desc: A ship of the line of the Black Sea Fleet, it participated in the defense of Sevastopol during the Crimean War. It was scuttled along with other ships on February 13, 1855, to block Anglo-French ships from entering the harbor. Fortunately, the Archipelago's shipwrights managed to recover its blueprints.
ship_56_desc: A marvelous invention of 1783. You can control altitude, speed, and direction, but cannot move against the wind. The balloon can be attacked and risks crashing into mountain terrain.
ship_57_desc: Named after the victory over the Swedish Empire at the Battle of Poltava, she was the first ship of the line to be laid down and built at the Saint Petersburg Admiralty. She features a "Dutch" transom and sculptures of Saint George as its decoration.
ship_58_desc: The French ship Unite was captured and commissioned into the Royal Navy as HMS Surprise. She was considered a hybrid between a corvette and a frigate. The Surprise became famous in 1799 when she recaptured HMS Hermione from the French.
ship_59_desc: The flagship of the Duke of Medina Sidonia, commander of the Spanish Armada. First launched in 1580, she played a crucial role in defending the Armada as she sailed through the English Channel. She survived the storm and English cannons to return safely to Spain.
ship_60_desc: A French galleass from the late 17th century. Based on the traditional Mediterranean galley, but built on a larger scale.
ship_61_desc: The flagship of Dutch Admiral de Ruyter, launched in 1665. A veteran of many victorious naval battles, the ship was named in honor of the seven autonomous provinces that formed the Dutch Republic in the 17th century.
ship_62_desc: A warship built by the Empire's shipwrights. It was first captured during a battle with the Cult of the Witnesses of the Apocalypse. According to the dying captain of the cultists, these ships were found drifting crewless somewhere in the Endless Sea.
ship_63_desc: A paddle-wheeled steamboat, found frozen in the northern ice of the Archipelago. No survivors were found, leaving the mystery of how it ended up there. Despite its revolutionary design, the inefficient, massive steam engine requires large amounts of coal, leaving the ship poorly armed and unsuited to harsh conditions.
ship_65_desc: Named after the Mayan god of wind - Huracan, this giant is a symbol of the Inhagger Empire's military might, a native civilization whose technological level rivals and often surpasses that of the Archipelago states.
ship_67_desc: German "great" frigate of the 17th century. On July 25, 1692, the Friedrich Wilhelm departed Emden for Guinea. During the voyage, it defeated a French vessel.
ship_68_desc: Line ship commissioned by Cardinal Richelieu as part of the naval reinforcement program. Participated in several battles and ultimately burned during the Battle of La Hougue after being attacked by English fireships.
ship_69_desc: Flagship of the Black Sea Fleet, hero of the Battle of Navarino. The first Russian ship awarded the St. George stern flag for victory over five Turkish ships.
ship_70_desc: A ship meant to be the pride of the Swedish Empire but became known for sinking on its maiden voyage. Severian shipbuilders fixed its design flaws and built a copy destined for glory in battle.
ship_71_desc: Norwegian gunboat schooner, one of ten built for the Dano-Norwegian fleet before the end of the Napoleonic Wars, when Norway became independent from Denmark under the Treaty of Kiel in 1814.

item_desc13: Increase ship defense by 2.5 but decrease maneuverability by 6%.
item_desc14: Increase ship defense by 4 and speeds up extinguishing minor fires but decrease maneuverability by 10%.

item_desc16: Reduces all damage by 25%, including for sails and crew. Disperses quickly

item_desc23: +25% damage for guns and mortars against structures, +30% to reload speed of swivel guns.

item_desc28: +0.5 knot to the speed of all ships in the squadron. Buff Distance: 500.
item_desc29: +7% to damage of all weapons of all ships in the squadron. Buff Distance: 500.
item_desc30: +7% damage and +1 knot to the speed of all ships in the squadron. Buff Distance: 500.
item_desc31: Adds 5% temporary durability, which absorbs damage, for all allies in the squadron.
item_desc32: You can place it anywhere away from ports (the icon will turn green if allowed), allowing all ships in your fleet in nearby waters to respawn there after sinking. Placing a new outpost will replace the old one. Other players can destroy it. Not available during Port Battles.
item_desc33: Crew will load guns 20% faster but work slower with the sails. Ship speed will be decreased by 8%.
item_desc34: The crew works more efficiently: Maneuverability +5%, reload speed of other items +30%, sail handling speed +10%, ammunition swap speed +30%, repair speed at sea +30%, item retrieval at sea +50%, ship speed in rowing mode increased by 1.5 knots.
item_desc35: Temporarily recover 500 durability, which absorbs damage.
item_desc40: Enhance any ammo type with phosphorus. Increases penetration by +2 and slows enemy ships on impact. Does not stack with gunpowder and reduces the cannon's angle of fire. Cannot be canceled.
item_desc41: Enhances any ammo type, causing it to explode and deal area damage. NPS are more vulnerable to explosions. Reduces attack range by 5% and halves penetration, but the damage is compensated by the explosion, which harms even protected ships.
item_desc42: Doubles the weapons received when sinking NPS.
item_desc43: When activated, the ship becomes fully invisible to others. NPS and towers will not attack it. Works at distances greater than 60 units.
item_desc44: When activated, squadmates can respawn near the ship within a 1000 radius after sinking, up to 20 times in total. Doesn't work during PB. While active, the ship receives 50% damage reduction, but cannot move at speed 3, and weapon reload is twice as slow.

mendingButtonTt: Repair of durability and sails, or fire extinguishing. Only performed at 1st speed. The amount of wood in the ship's hold is shown above the icon.
ship_stat_tt_1: Durability is the key parameter that keeps your ship afloat. Greater durability means more expensive repairs. The durability of the sails is directly linked to the ship's overall durability.
ship_stat_tt_2: Speed is a maximum combat speed. The ship can go at three speeds: the first increases maneuverability and reduces speed, the second is the main combat speed, and the third is cruise speed, which increases speed but reduces maneuverability. Speed is affected by upgrades and how loaded the hold is.
ship_stat_tt_3: Armor - the thickness of the side cloth, which reduces damage from cannonballs. If the penetration of a cannon is 10 and the armor is 2, then the ship will take 8 points of damage * ammo type damage and effects. At an angle, the thickness increases, which reduces damage. The bow is more sturdy and the stern is less armored
ship_stat_tt_4: Hold - maximum weight of goods and equipment that can be transported
ship_stat_tt_5: Crew - the number of places for people who participate in boarding battles, load guns and help perform various actions. A ship with a large crew is more difficult to board or disable
ship_stat_tt_6: The larger the ship relative to others, the easier it is to hit it
ship_stat_tt_7: Mortars are large caliber weapons that use their own ammunition. To fire: hold the [{0}] button to aim at the target, and then LMB to fire. Mortars cannot attack targets that are close. Maximum caliber on this ship: {1} in.
ship_stat_tt_8: maneuverability - the angle at which the ship can turn in 10 seconds on the second sail speed
ship_stat_tt_10: The ship has a space to install a special weapon (for example, greek fire)
ship_stat_tt_11: Unique sail armament will allow traversal under sharp angles towards the wind, surpassing other ships. However, when going directly against the wind, the sails will block each other and speed will decrease
ship_stat_tt_12: Oars allow you to greatly increase movement speed against the wind
ship_stat_tt_13: The heavier the ship is, the more difficult it is for it to gain speed and complete maneuvers. The success of a ramming attack mostly depends on speed and mass

personal_isle_open: Here you can claim your own personal safe island, construct buildings on it, which allow you to produce and store resources, change ships, send out expeditions and more. Currently you have {0} out of {1} max. islands
personal_isle_open_err: You already have the maximum amount of personal islands {0}/{1}
next_build_cost: Each next constructed building will be more expensive
personal_isle_b1_tt: Allows you to change and repair ships (durability and integrity) while on the island. Unlocks access to the arena and modes.
personal_isle_b3_tt: Allows you to craft a chosen resource, just like in a port, but at a lower cost by using the island's worker resources instead of crafting time.
personal_isle_b4_tt: The base storage limit on the island increases to 500t. You can store any goods, and even leave prisoners - here they will be under watch.
personal_isle_b5_tt: Adds the ability to send an expedition to collect produced resources from nearby mines and manufactories (within {0} distance) and deliver them to the island. Each expedition is sent manually by clicking the island icon on the World Map and uses {1} pts. of manpower.
personal_isle_b6_tt: Allows you to produce one of the following resources: grain, sugar, beer, supplies. Consumes 3.5 of manpower per hour, while active.
personal_isle_b7_tt: Random sailors will visit and stay overnight. You can get rumors and quest markers for free. Manpower resource recovery +1/hour
personal_isle_b8_tt: The bright and towering lighthouse illuminates the island on the mini-map, enables fast travel to nearby ports (if you have the skill), and allows you to reach the island from any port in the world.
personal_isle_6_inside: You can refit the establishment or pause/resume operations. When the establishment is idle, no manpower is consumed.
personal_isle_6_change: Refit the establishment to produce a different resource. Refit cost - {0} units of labor resources. Options:
personal_isle_3_inside: Refit the workshop to produce a different resource. Refit cost - {0} units of labor resources. Options:
personal_isle_forMonets_ask: Restore the island's entire manpower for {0} coins?
personal_isle_leave_accept: Leaving the island will remove all buildings and storage, but you'll be free to claim another island. Abandon island?
personal_isle_leave_res_warning: Captain! There are resources left in the island's storage. Collect them before leaving, or they'll be lost. All buildings and storage will be removed, but you'll be free to claim another island. Abandon island?

action_ArenaRewards_desc: Arena rewards in gold and experience are tripled today. Play smart to earn more!
action_arenaTorunament_ingame_desc: Compete in "Tournament Duel" mode with ships of {0} rate (rate changes weekly) to become an arena legend and win great prizes!$$The tournament last one week. Each participant starts with a base score; the winner takes part of points of the defeated. You will battle only with players in the league same as you. The current rating, awards and leagues can be found in the tournament tab$$Mon-Wed: warm-up. No special rules$Thu-Fri: Lose a life upon falling to the lowest league$Sat-Sun: Lose a life upon dropping to a lower league$*Initial lives: {1}
action_discount1_desc: 15% discount on items for coins in the Shop section
action_discount2_desc: 20% discount on items and designs for coins in the Shop section
action_discount3_desc: 10% discount on any Captain’s Seal in the Shop section
action_discount4_desc: 10% discount on any large chest and treasures in the Store
action_empire_desc: News has arrived! Under the cover of night, a few hours before the attack window, the Empire's fleet will launch an assault... Their goal is to reclaim territories from the factions. Each guild must defend their port by destroying all Imperial ships around their fort. Port battles will also be available during the event
action_tender_desc: The Great Sultan Mahmet ibn Khanfar, may his name be praised, is ready to bestow his favor upon the best trade guild in the Archipelago and grant the title of Sea Vizier for 10 days and nights. To earn the Shah's favor, the guild must deliver the most "Amber." Time limit: 24 hours. $$Amber can be purchased in limited quantities at any port (even without a guild), but only sold at the Trade House in Halij Albahrad by trade guilds to earn points. After the event, all Amber will disappear
action_pirateDay_desc: Load your cannons and prepare for battle! Only today, bonuses for black and pirate flags are higher, and PvP sinkings grant more Battle Marks and experience. Each sinking earns you a black mark!
action_portTensity_desc: Today, guilds can raise tensions at enemy ports much faster.
action_legendary_desc: The capital governors have agreed to eliminate all dangerous ships. Today, you'll earn double rewards for sinking Imperial Legends!
action_tradingWar_desc: Goods prices are fluctuating! Due to frequent port battles, many traders are rushing to unload or sell goods quickly to avoid losses. Watch prices and seize the opportunity!
action_StrongFog_desc: A thick fog, reducing visibility, has drifted into the Archipelago from the Endless Sea.
action_WaterDrought_desc: Water consumption is faster, and purchase prices at ports are higher. For some, this is a crisis; for others, a chance to profit...
action_Cooling_desc: All sawmills are operating slower. Now's the time to seek alternative ways to obtain wood or shift production.
action_Epidemy_desc: Widespread illness has made crew recruitment difficult and slowed farm production. Crew on ships is also affected...
action_SpecificResourceDeficit_desc: Cities across the world lack this resource. Use the shortage to your advantage: buy from traders, build mines and workshops, or even raid routes carrying it.
action_RareTraders_desc: Hurry to buy scarce goods or sell blueprint fragments at a good price. Ferryman ships will transport coastal brotherhood buyers. Remember - you can only buy once, but you can fill your hold.
action_BEmpireShipbuilderInPort_desc: In the port of "{0}," Galdos de la Galledo, apprentice to the legendary Maurice Fleury, offers his services. A man who learned the secrets of imperial shipbuilding before fleeing to the Archipelago. Don't miss the chance to build an imperial ship of any rank! He visits a new city every three days but never stays in coves.
action_randomPvpArena_desc: Captain, test your battle instincts and earn gold! The Sea Skirmish lasts 20 minutes. Every second, players earn {0} gold. The more rivals, the less gold-since it's split among all! Be the last one standing to claim it all! Conditions: The Sea Skirmish occurs every 1-3 hours and lasts 20 minutes. Event zone - a buoy circle where gold is awarded.
action_update_release_desc: You may encounter bugs and unfinished elements, but fixes are on the way. We value your feedback: if any changes don't resonate with the community, they'll be revisited. Check out the changes in official communities: {0}
MerchantInTheSeaNearThePort: to a merchant at sea near {0}

DailyChallenge_Pvp1: Sink 3 players from enemy factions in open world. If unguilded, any players in guilds. Enemy ships must not be weaker than yours.
DailyChallenge_Pvp2: Sink 3 players from enemy factions controlling ports.
DailyChallenge_Pvp4: Sink 2 players who are sailing under Black or Pirate flags
DailyChallenge_Pvp5: Sail a specified distance without using peace flags (or any PvP protection). You can do this while completing other quests or activities.

DailyChallenge_Newyear: Sink 5 ships in PvP using fireworks for the final shot
GrabbingTrader_0: Based on the logbook, a merchant ship ran aground at this location. However, the whereabouts of the entire crew are unclear... The last entry was made 6 days ago. Well then, let's search the ship and find something valuable.
GrabbingTrader_1: A voice from the deck: "Hey! After the attack, we docked here. Move along; our backup is on its way." Loot the ship or leave?
GrabbingTrader_2: This spot seems untouched, with a whole ship intact. Might be worth taking a look inside...
PersonFromShip_0: Captain! My ship ran aground! If only I could reach port... I'll repay your help as soon as I get assistance!
PersonFromShip_1: Help! My ship was fired upon by freebooters and docked on shore. Please help me and my crew reach port. We'll make your deed known!
FindLoot_desc_0: After what happened here... I don't want pirates interested in these waters. Captain PLAYER_NAME! Gather the crates and debris scattered at sea and report back.
FindLoot_desc_1: After the recent attack on a trade squadron, they say there are still many crates and debris at sea. Gather them quickly, and stay out of sight!
FindLoot_desc_2: Captain PLAYER_NAME! One of the trade company ships attacked had important mail for me. My gratitude will be swift if you can find it among the debris. I'll wait here...
FindLoot_desc_3: After the last storm, it seems a lot of extra debris washed into these waters. Gather whatever you can find and report back.
