worldEmpireFort_tt: An abandoned fort where the Empire stores its resources for an assault. Brave captains can attack, destroy, and loot it: the reward increases over time.
war_comp_err1: War Campaign quest available again in {0} hrs.
war_comp_err2: You must be in a guild group at this port
transfQ_toTrader1: Ah, PLAYER_NAME, times are rough! My goods {0} are barely selling here due to recent events. I'll trade in the shop near the crossroads. Will you handle the transport?
transfQ_toTrader2: With good weather lately, production's up and storage is nearly full. Help transport the surplus {0} before it goes to waste.
transfQ_toTrader3: Good day, Captain PLAYER_NAME. Can you deliver {0} to the settlement? My goods are of the finest quality and worth every coin! What do you say?
transfQ_toTrader4: Captain, I've heard my goods won't be accepted in this port. I know a reliable merchant willing to pay. Deliver {0} as soon as possible!
transfQ_toTrader5: (You enter the cabin and encounter a drunk with a strong odor) Ah, Captain! Help with my troubles... hic. My ship's wrecked... hic... and I've got an urgent delivery! I need {0} delivered fast! Save my... hic reputation, Captain.
transfQ_toPort1: Power and money, that's all that's valued these days! You've got plenty of power, Captain PLAYER_NAME, but I can help with the money. So, a good acquaintance of mine is waiting for a shipment of {0} in the glorious city of {1}, but lately, there's been a lack of volunteers, and honest captains are a rarity in these parts.
transfQ_toPort2: Greetings, Captain PLAYER_NAME! Help deliver {0} to {1}. My goods are of the finest quality and worth every coin. What do you say?
transfQ_toPort3: Captain, I see you're about to set sail. Allow me to offer you a job. You need to transport {0} to {1}, and the sooner the better. I'll pay extra for the urgency!
transfQ_toPort4: Would you like wine or rum, Captain PLAYER_NAME? You might earn some gold. A merchant came to me recently, looking for a captain who could quickly deliver {0}. He's reliable. Take the job, money never hurts!
transfQ_toPort5: We urgently need help! With recent piracy near {1}, our small trade guild must pay insurance. We've agreed to cover it with goods... Now {0} needs transport, but everyone's busy! You know how it is, right?
transfQ_toPort6: Finally, a brave captain in these parts! Brave souls are rare these days - cursed weather. If you've got the courage, I need {0} transported. You seem capable of delivering it safely.
transfQ_smug1: There's a job to move stolen goods {0}. Don't worry, no one will know!
transfQ_smug2: Ha-ha, bet you're the type to take risks for profit? Look, I need these crates {0} delivered. Truth be told, they were obtained through... unofficial means, but that's a minor detail, right?
transfQ_smug3: I have a cargo that needs delivering {0}. Don't ask questions; I'm grateful to those who help! Rumor has it prices will soon skyrocket, and I'll be able to sell it quietly.
transfQ_smug4: This is strictly between us. In the coming days, an attack on a trade fleet is planned. Where and when? I can't say. They need all the supplies we can get. A fair price for this service. So, do we have a deal?
transfQ_smug5: Greetings, PLAYER_NAME. My friend's small shop urgently needs supplies. The goods... well, aren't entirely legal... You understand. A man at the docks will be waiting with payment upon your arrival.
transfQ_toTraderAppendix: The location will be marked on the map near {0}.
transfQ_passengers_0: Captain, will you take the transport job? See that group of {0} people? They need to get to {1} and are willing to buy passage. Shall I invite them aboard?
transfQ_passengers_1: Greetings, Captain! I'm leading a group of {0} workers needing transport to {1}. I heard your ship's heading there. Will you take us? We'll pay handsomely.
transfQ_passengers_2: Ahoy on the bridge! Are you the captain here? By chance, are you heading to {1}? I'll pay in gold if you'll take me and my {0} men there.
QuestRiotAndRevenge_0: Ahoy, PLAYER_NAME. Here's the problem - my first mate turned out to be a real rat. He was planning a mutiny but couldn't pull it off. When it all came to light, he ran off with part of the crew. I want to teach him a lesson! He won't let me near him, but you, on the other hand, just might. What do you say? I'll pay you his annual wages! And I'll throw in a bonus if you bring him back alive. I want to look him in the eyes before I send him under the keel...
QuestRiotAndRevenge_1: Unknown intruders seized my ship right here! They snuck aboard in the moonlight, sneaking up on deck. Board the ship and bring those men back to me!
QuestKillWithSailes_0: Many traitors have appeared among the pirates. They're gathering information, hiding treasures... I suspect they're up to something. I want them brought to justice. Before sinking their ships, destroy their sails down to {0}%!
QuestKillWithSailes_1: There's work to be done. We've been tracking a few ships for some time now. It seems they're under the command of fanatics. They're preparing for attacks, but we'll catch them off guard. Don't let them slip away! Before sinking their ships, destroy their sails down to {0}%!
QuestDestroyTraders_0: Psst, Psst, captain, fancy earning some coin? I won't bore you with details - wouldn't understand them anyway. We need some ruckus in nearby waters - sink a couple of merchant ships. There are some folks here who keep crossing our path, but we can't say exactly who they are. Catch my drift? Just do what they ask. What's the term... Ah, mercenary work, that's it. You do it, we pay.
QuestDestroyTraders_1: Well, look who's here! It's PLAYER_NAME! How's life at sea? Remember how to fire a cannon or a pistol? Good, I've got a job for you. Heard the merchants recently changed their route? No? Well, let's give them a little fright, make them think it's safer elsewhere. All the cargo in the holds is yours, plus we'll throw in some gold for your service. All fair and square.
QuestDestroyTraders_2: Hey, PLAYER_NAME! Just got word a convoy of trade ships is passing nearby. Who could refuse easy pickings? Exactly - no one! So we need to move fast.
Challenge_NoDamage_0: Captain! A friend and I have a wager going: can you sink a ship without taking any damage? I say you can, but he doesn't believe it! This whole "anti...ani...antiquity" era was all about clean shots at sea, so says the drunken archivist from Bord Radel! One shot and that's it. Prove him wrong, and we split the winnings! I'd do it myself, but my ship's in the docks for repairs...
Challenge_NoDamage_1: Hey! We need to destroy a smuggler ship coming into port, and we need to spread rumors that a strong privateer did it. So you can't take any damage while sinking them!
CaptureAndDeliverPirate_0: Several patrol ships have been seized by pirates and are heading out to sea. You must reclaim at least one of these vessels through boarding!
CaptureAndDeliverPirate_1: Greetings! The governor ordered the rebels to be eliminated. I can still hear his shout: "Such vile traitors and scoundrels, I've never seen!" So, you need to deal with these troublemakers and escort their ships. Every plank is precious these days.
CaptureAndDeliverPirate_hard: A patrol ship was captured last night. Board it, and return the ship to the port. Be prepared - other pirates may attack you!
QuestDestroyPirates_0: Greetings, captain PLAYER_NAME. I already know why you are here - people do not come to us to have tea with the governor. Let's not beat around the bush: You do the job, and we pay. Ever since these territory wars began, the sea's been dangerous for regular merchants. There's a gang of raiders here causing us trouble. Disguise as a merchant and sink them before they sink you. Agreed?
QuestDestroyPirates_1: Blast those smugglers to blazes! They're... Arrgh! Captain, your timing couldn't be better. Ready for a new job? Some filthy smugglers are hauling goods from our harbor, stuffing them in every nook and cranny of their ship! We lost the value of three warships last week, and even more this week! I swear, I'll pay handsomely for every one of them that dares haul cargo through our waters!
QuestDestroyPirates_2: Greetings, PLAYER_NAME! We've got a job for you. This order is to sink pirate ships, these are with the black flags, and secure the trade routes. Not only will this bring prosperity to the city, but it's a fine chance to raise your reputation!
QuestDestroyPirates_3: I'm responsible for patrolling these waters. There's a group of pirates in the neighboring seas. We don't have enough ships to secure our waters, so there's no one to send after them. Just keep quiet about it, alright? I'd handle it myself, but I can't leave the patrol. Do us a favor, head there and deal with the pirates.
AgainstLegend_0: Heard about you, PLAYER_NAME! So, at these coordinates there is a dangerous criminal. Your task is to find him, board him, and then deliver him to the port.
AgainstLegend_1: I heard how you dealt with the pirates. Impressive! It's time to target someone even bigger. Head to the nearby waters, find the dangerous criminal, and board his ship. Be cautious! He's heavily armed and prepared.
KillFerry_0: Captain, I have an urgent mission! We recently learned that a large merchant ship has been seized and is headed for a bay. The cargo on board is poisoned. Find it and sink it! Keep in mind, if you're seen, people might misunderstand, and your reputation will suffer. But don't worry - the reward will be worth it.
KillFerry_1: Hello, PLAYER_NAME. Erm... Let's agree this meeting is secret; no one should know. There's a person... who did something very wrong! If you're willing to risk your reputation a little to make them pay... I'll reward you handsomely. That's all, off you go.
QuestKillWithSailes_Caper_0: Got a job for you. Several merchant ships have recently been captured and are now being used for some mysterious cargo runs in the Endless Sea. Before sinking them, you need to damage their sails by 66% to prevent any escape.
QuestKillWithSailes_Caper_1: My organization's been taking losses lately. It's hard times, no one believes in a bright future anymore... We need to sink these ships - they'll mistake you for an ally. Yes, it's drastic, but necessary. First, damage their sails to 66%.
QuestPowderKeg_0: Here's the plan, PLAYER_NAME: at dawn, a few ships led by former convicts plan to cause some trouble. You need to get close and set them off with a powder barrel. Whether you finish these scoundrels off is up to you!
QuestPowderKeg_1: We need to launch an attack on these ships as quickly as possible! They must not reach their destination. Others are already searching for them. Oh, this is going to be big... You'll need a fast ship to set them off with a mine or powder barrel. You don't have to finish them, just get out of the fight.
QuestKillAmbusher_0: Word on the wind is there's a new threat: pirates have set up an ambush and are troubling small merchants, who are barely getting by! They're likely near the mines or private islands nearby if they haven't already fled. Get on this quickly!
QuestKillAmbusher_1: Pirates have ambushed the settlements again! Once we have their coordinates, you'll see it marked on your map, giving you the opportunity to go after them. Your mission is to move quickly and put these sea scoundrels down to make the seas safer. Check the mines and private islands in these waters! If they're gone, you're too late...
QuestDeliveryGuild: To support city development, goods need to be transported as part of a squadron. You must deliver {0}x {1} to {2}. The total cargo weight is {3}t. Ensure the cargo arrives safely! To accept the quest, the guild must invest 1 influence point!
educ_holdtt: Press [{0}] to see what's now in the hold
educ_no_pvp: No PvP protection (other players can attack)
sacrafice_desc: After the cataclysm, religious fanatics emerged, believing that it was a prophecy that came true and that the archipelago was the last refuge for humankind. They perform strange rituals, seemingly hoping to find salvation by sacrificing those who don't share their beliefs.
sacrafice_desc_b2: You will receive a black mark
PortCompanyWindow_10: You sit down with a group of merchants. Surely, they have work related to cargo transport. The conversation halts, and all eyes turn to you. Call the waiter to treat everyone and break the ice?
PortCompanyWindow_11: Officers drink at separate tables in a corner. Sharing your tales of battle might land you a job. Call the waiter to treat everyone and loosen some tongues?
PortCompanyWindow_12: The tavern's patrons look like a gallery - pirates marked by scars, missing limbs, or eyes. A card game unfolds by the window, and rumors of some pirate's adventures are hotly debated at the bar. Join the conversation?
PortCompanyWindow_19: No more rumors or work for now
PortCompanyWindow_39: Faction may have work for you
radnom_meet_text: During conversations, you meet a professional who could join your crew
recruit_text: Surely among the island's travelers, you could find someone of interest for your crew
PortEnteringSettingsWindow_1: Auto-restocking is missing some items in the warehouse
PortEnteringSettingsWindow_4: If there's enough gold, repairs will be carried out
PortEnteringSettingsWindow_7: All cargo and resources from the hold (except ammunition) will be moved to the warehouse
PortEnteringSettingsWindow_9: When transferring, wood and cloth will be left behind
PortEnteringSettingsWindow_16_b: Don't ask if there's enough gold
PortRealShopPage_102: Get ships, designs, $items, and more! Hover to view.
chest_new25_desc: Early access to ships with guaranteed drop.
PortRealShopPage_104: Instantly get 3 guaranteed different chest rewards. Cheaper than opening one chest 3 times!
PortRealShopPage_108: Restore craft-time limit $with a boost, receiving +1000 hours
PortRealShopPage_110: +5 ranks* $+30,000 gold $+50 scrolls $Ships with cannons: $Le Cerf Cutter, Polacca, $Russia, Anson
PortRealShopPage_112: +5 ranks* $+15,000 gold $+100 Battle Marks $Ship with cannons: $Black Prince
PortRealShopPage_114: +4 ranks* $+15,000 gold $+50 mortar shells $Ship with cannons: $Golden Apostle
PortRealShopPage_116: +3 ranks* $+15,000 gold $Ship with cannons: $Phoenix $ $Available for ranks below 18
PortRealShopPage_1001: +30,000 gold $Ship Bellona $Set of guns "28-pdr Carronade" $7 top upgrades to the warehouse
PortRealShopPage_1002: +30,000 gold $Ship Victory $Set of guns "32-pdr Long Cannon" $7 top upgrades to the warehouse
PortRealShopPage_cannonskit_d: +30,000 gold $+40 guns $"22-pdr Scorcher" $+20,000 XP across all branches
PortRealShopPage_121: 2000x Saxon Shot, 50x Phosphorous Shots and 10x Phosphorous Mines
PortRealShopPage_128: +{0} hours of Peace Flag activation. Available everywhere without additional mooring fees. Not functional in the Endless Sea. Time counts only when activated.
PortRealShopPage_132: +{0} days of recovery and crafting time limit at the port +50%. All mining and manufacturing speeds +50%. Recovers crafting time when purchased!
PortRealShopPage_136: For {0} minutes (time counts at sea), your hold will be hidden and won't be lost on sinking or boarding
PortRealShopPage_137: Island Caravan: transport resources to any chosen port
PortRealShopPage_138: Hire Caravan: transport resources to another selected port
PortRealShopPage_139: Before purchase, you will be prompted to select a destination port. Up to 1000t of resources can be transferred at once.
PortRealShopPage_141: You haven't placed anything in the hold
PortRealShopPage_142: You're about to move {0} units of resources totaling {1} tons to {2}
caravan_in_reward: +1 Free caravan (move all resources to any city of your choice)
PortRealShopPage_crew_chest_desc: Select a category to attract 5 random specialists:
donate_caravan_step1: Before choosing a port, place all resources you want to deliver in the current ship's hold. Currently, there are {0} units weighing {1} tons.
donate_caravan_step2: Choose the port to transport resources to:
donate_caravan_fail: Cannot transport resources weighing over {0} tons.
flag_trader_tooltip: Low risk: protection near #waters around ports or personal #islands. These decoy flags have no penalties: access all cities, carry overloads. Loot bonus applies to ship and fort plunder.
flag_pirate_tooltip: A flag for daring captains: PvP mode is always on. Faction players cannot use this flag. Loot bonus applies to ship and fort plunder.
flag_war_2_trader: +20% XP and rewards for Trade Union missions
flag_war_tooltip: Core faction flags: PvP is always active, but they give bonuses - missions for trade guilds, loot for others. Loot bonus applies to ship and fort plunder.
flag_caper_tooltip: The "no flags" mode allows ambushes and evasion: nickname and guild tag are hidden. Port towers attack after 40 seconds in the area. Guild and trusted allies, or fleet members, can identify you by your green hull bar. Unlock for all ships by building at least one rank 1 ship.
flag_legend_2: +33% more marks for battles in the world
pb_tower_tt: Destroy towers to initiate a fort siege or force the owner into lengthy repairs. During PB, towers can even be captured!
pb_fort_tt: Destroying or raiding a fort reduces the city level. For final capture, all towers must be destroyed, and infantry must be deployed at the fort.
guildMember_refill: Replenishments for forts' storages and the guild's vault
guildMember_conquerorpart: Contribution to influence missions, calculated into influence points. For example, if a member earns 40 achievements out of 80 needed, this will add 0.5 to the stats.
GuildWindow_SyncProtected_6: These guilds will be allies for yours! You can join PBs together, their guild tag will appear green, and your buildings and patrols will not attack them. To activate the rule, both guilds must mark each other as trusted. The rule takes effect immediately but can only be removed after {0} minutes.
GuildWindow_SyncProtected_21: Removal at season's end due to inactivity
guildLog_DisputeVoting: {0} voted to expel [{1}] from the faction
kingshipDisallowedByTitle: In this faction, only guilds with gold and silver titles can vote for expulsion
dispute_guild_info_voting: {0} out of {1} votes for expulsion have been cast against the guild [{2}]. Your guild has a weight of {3} votes, which you can add
dispute_guild_info_kingship: {0} out of {1} guilds with gold and silver titles voted to expel [{2}]. You can add 1 vote
already_voted: You have already voted to expel this guild
fraction_dispute_voting: Expulsion requires a general vote with the required number of votes
fraction_dispute_kingship: Expulsion requires {0} guilds with gold and silver titles to vote in favor
desc_mine_tt: Gradually produces resources that fill its internal storage, even when you're offline.
desc_farm_tt: The farm raises animals, even when you're offline. The more animals, the faster they reproduce (work speed increases).
education_quest_not_opened: Complete the "Coastal Brotherhood" mission to unlock
education_quest_1_text: Welcome to the Archipelago! That was bold to defending our trade ships, especially on such a... vessel. $$ I believe you can handle our problems. Leave port immediately and attack the nearest pirate ship! By the sea gods, it’s sitting in the bay for a reason, scouting our patrol routes!
startq_action_1_1: Exit the port [Space] and sink an NPS with black flags.
education_quest_2_text: I told you, you'd manage! Aye?! I see your sailors managed to salvage some junk from the hold of that sinking pirate. My advice? Don't haul it across the seas - sell it right away at the local market. See the sign "Trading House"? These merchants buy and sell just about everything across the Archipelago...
startq_action_2_1: Check what's left - open "Hold and Warehouse" in the left panel
startq_action_2_2: Buy ammunition from the Trading House
startq_action_2_4: Sell meat/grain at the Trading House.
education_quest_3_text: Cannonballs are great - but you've got to do something about your boat... Let me introduce you to the local craftsmen. With gold and materials, they work wonders. The ship becomes more agile, the guns more accurate... Even fish and meat in the hold lasts longer.
startq_action_3_1: Install an upgrade on your ship
startq_action_3_2: Equip the consumables in the "Hold and Warehouse" window
education_quest_4_text: Another pirate vessel has been has been spotted. By the looks of it, she is hunting for her old companion, already resting on the seabed. Do not keep her waiting! Take your upgraded ship and help her find her friend. The items will help you patch the holes or strengthen your broadside.
startq_action_4_1: Attack the ship and use a consumable during battle ([X], [C], or [V] key)
education_quest_5_text: I tip my hat to you, PLAYER_NAME! Fine fighting there. Not sure if I mentioned it... but here in the shallows it's calm, beyond that - you'll be an easy target. Time to trade that sloop for something better!
startq_action_5_1: Build the ship {0} at the Shipyard. Buy missing resources at the Trading House or gather them in the world.
startq_action_5_2: Buy more rusty cannons in the "Weapons" tab at the Workshop
education_quest_6_text: Not bad, not bad at all... Who knows, you might command a great line ship one day. That was my dream once... $$ Now, hire a crew and arm your new vessel. Don't spend all your gold on the best cannons just yet, you'll need it!
startq_action_6_2: Move your crew to the new ship
education_quest_7_text: PLAYER_NAME, rumors say those pirates are hunting you now! Bold, aren't they? Visit the Tavern and talk to people. You might learn where pirates are setting an ambush - or find escape routes via merchant contacts. Your call. You'll also earn some reputation and coin.
startq_action_7_1: Hear rumors in the Tavern and complete a quest
education_quest_8_text: Time to prepare - those pirates will strike again. I don't know who's behind them... but you'll need new ships. And wouldn't you know - all the wood in town's been bought up. You'll have to go to a nearby port. Use "Fast Travel" to move quickly, then bring the wood back.
startq_action_8_1: Move to a nearby port ("Weigh anchor" - "Fast Travel")
startq_action_8_2: Buy 200 wood at the Trading House and load it into the hold
education_quest_t1_text: Hello PLAYER_NAME. I saw you in the Tavern and heard from the officers... in our small town, everyone sees everything. Maybe it's not my business, but I simply can't let you stay on that wreck. Who'll protect us then? You need resources for a new ship, and luckily for you, I'll show you how to earn them honestly. Let's start with beams.
startq_action_t1_1: Open the special workshop (Port - Workshop - Beam)
startq_action_t1_2: Craft 10 beams and leave them in storage
education_quest_t2_text: Ships break, upgrades wear out, weapons runs out, and the poor crew can die in battle, from disease, or hunger. At the very least, you must learn how to provide for yourself and even earn by trading.
startq_action_t2_1: Collect {0} gold and build any production (look for the pickaxe marker on the map)
startq_action_t2_2: Make tackles in the Workshop and go fishing (look for the fish icon)
startq_action_t2_3: Find a small island and land to search for resources (look for the shovel icon)
education_quest_t3_text: All resources are gathered or produced by someone on the Archipelago. Many are in short supply, especially with all these endless wars... In that case, trade is your best option. You should definitely check out the local markets and merchants.
startq_action_t3_1: Buy resources at a good price from a sea trader or fishing village
startq_action_t3_2: Buy resources in one port, sell in another and profit on the difference
EducationGui_GameTT_MoveResourcesWithout: You'll have to sail the resources back - ferries only work with empty holds. Time to head back!
account_delete_err_guild: To delete your account, you must leave the guild
startq_action_t3_3: Collect resources from a previously built production
education_quest_t4_text: It was a brilliant idea by the Trade Union to open Auctions in towns. Captains come, post their goods, even ships. In times of shortage, even small lots sell fast.
startq_action_t4_1: Buy any lot from the Auction in the Trade House (e.g. part of the materials for the desired ship)
startq_action_t4_2: Sell any item at the Auction in the Trade House
education_quest_t5_text: Much better! Now I feel the future is in safe hands. Let's see what you can do with all those resources.
startq_action_t5_1: Build a warehouse in any port
eq_extra_4: Permanent bonus +{0}% experience in ship branches (!)
education_quest_b1_text: Pirates are cunning. Be ready for boarding - they may sneak up and seize your ship without a fight. A few folks in the Tavern are ready to bring you loyal fighters.
startq_action_b1_1: Hire musketeers, mercenaries, or soldiers for your ship
education_quest_b2_text: You could win a boarding fight. But your task is tougher - capture an entire ship! Then either scrap it in port plank by plank, or use it as an ally in battle. Your choice.
startq_action_b2_1: Damage the crew using Grape Shot
startq_action_b2_2: Win a boarding fight against an NPS and capture its ship
education_quest_b3_text: Remember: ship bows are well-armored, sterns are weak. A sinking ship can sometimes keep fighting. Also, speed matters - a great way to catch foes off guard is to disable their sails.
startq_action_b3_1: Deliver a finishing blow to a ship by firing at the stern
startq_action_b3_2: Shred the sails with bar shot down to 90%
education_quest_b6_text: I can't help you any further - the pirates know you're here and are already hunting. You'll have to leave the city, and build a better ship while you're at it - our shipyard is too small. I'm sure you'll manage! I'll send a few good cannons your way. Until we meet again.
startq_action_6_3: Install an upgrade on the new ship
education_quest_b7_text: Congrats on your new home! PLAYER_NAME, I've told you all I know... Now go polish your skills in the Arena, against others like you. It's an easy way to test yourself in battle without risk - and even earn something.
startq_action_b7_2: Play $$ battles in any Arena mode
education_quest_b8_text: Don't get too comfy in your new home - pirates will find you even here. It's important to find allies: a guild or fellow captains. If you haven't yet - now's the time. I don't follow faction politics anymore... too old for that. But I believe you'll make the right choice.
startq_action_b8_1: Open the guilds window (apply if desired)
startq_action_b8_2: Create or join a squadron (look for people nearby or invite friends)
startq_action_8_3: Learn a skill in the "{0}" category
eq_extra_1: Access to the tavern in ports (!)
eq_extra_3: Access to fast travel between ports (!)
eq_extra_all: The next game stage will unlock (!)
onboarding_education_start_1: The map has led you here. The marked location is near an island... Get closer to it.
onboarding_education_start_2: Press [{0}] and [{1}] to raise and lower sails.
onboarding_education_start_3: Wind direction can be determined by flags or the #arrow near the ship.
onboarding_education_battle1: You found nothing but wreckage. Looks like it's a trap. A #pirate #ship emerging from behind the island has mistaken you for an easy target. Prove them wrong!
onboarding_education_battle2: Aim at the ship and press #LMB. Try to hit the hull with some lead.
onboarding_education_battle3: #Wreckage often remains at the site of a sunken ship, containing valuable resources.
onboarding_education_battle4: After a battle, patch up your ship to avoid becoming fish food. Use wood from your hold for repairs, but repairs can only be done with sails lowered or at the first speed.
onboarding_education_battle5: You've found heated shots and bar shots. Press [1], [2], or [3] once to load the current side, and twice to load all sides.
onboarding_education_battle6: Something’s happening on the horizon… There’s an old spyglass in the hold. Time to use it.
onboarding_education_battle7: Ships with a "red mast" will attack on sight. Stay vigilant and keep an eye on the horizon.
onboarding_education_battle8: Looks like trade-flagged ships also fell into the trap. We might cooperate with them for profit.
onboarding_education_battle9: Now you can search the wreckage. #Loot depends on the ship: pirates carry gold, traders - resources.
onboarding_education_battle9_q: Inspect the wreckage in the sea
onboarding_education_back1: You’ve found gold, which is the main currency. You can see your amount in the top left corner. Gold cannot be lost, unlike resources in the hold
onboarding_education_back2: To check your #hold contents, press [{0}]. All ship resources are shown there.
onboarding_education_back3: The weather is worsening rapidly. There's a port nearby - it's time to head there. Use the minimap, deliver your loot, and settle in.
onboarding_education_back4: In cruising mode, your ship gradually gains extra #speed and reaches destinations faster. You’re almost there!
boarding_education_1: Click on the red target to $stop attacking this group$and deal more damage to others
boarding_education_2: Click on the shield to $protect the group from damage
boarding_education_3: Click on the grey target to $order an attack on this group
onboarding_port0: You’ve received {0} days of Captain’s Seal as a reward, speeding up your progress
onboarding_port1: The port is like your own home. Here, you are safe and able to build and change ships, trade resources and much more
onboarding_port1_q: Hold left or right mouse button to rotate the camera and look around
onboarding_port2: On the left side is information about the ship. The buttons with icons are responsible for main options - repair, hold, equipment, crew.
onboarding_port3: A map was found in one of the holds of the sunken ships. It's time to take a look at her and find out your whereabouts ([M] button)
onboarding_port8: Every captain charts their own course. You are free to roam the seas or follow a plan - write your own story. To leave the port, press [Space] or "Weigh anchor".
onboarding_port10_q: Talk with the man on the dock
EducationGui_49: You’re in the open world with thousands of ships. Ships with blue durability are NPS (Non-player ships); the rest belong to real players.
EducationGui_49_b: Sinking NPS ships is a great way to find gold and resources. Use the spyglass [{0}] to identify a pirate ship by its flag and sink it.
EducationGui_51: Some resources are needed to build ships and cast cannons: wood, iron, copper. Better to #save them.
EducationGui_53: The bow of the ship is stronger than the stern, and a thick, protected side is most effectively penetrated at a flat angle. A shot in the stern immediately finishes the sinking ship!
EducationGui_66: Mortars require their own type of ammunition: buy it from the local trading house or take it from warehouse.
EducationGui_67: Unique mortars require preparation before firing: lower all sails to begin. Once the bar is filled, you will be able to fire.
EducationGui_68: You can switch flags in port to Peace or Decoy to avoid PvP, or to Black to increase loot!
EducationGui_71_t: In the Arena you can take part in quick battles with other captains, earn special currency and rewards, and raise your ranking. Ship repairs are free.
EducationGui_72_t: Take at least 50-100 units of wood to repair your ship at sea after battles.
EducationGui_55: Each new rank grants skill points that can be used for development. Click the captain's icon to open the naval career window.
EducationGui_80: Quest marks have appeared on the world map. You can view and choose destinations in advance. Markers disappear over time.
EducationGui_81: This port can be exited from a nearby lighthouse, like one located at the other side of the port. This is convenient, but you can return only by going back to the port itself, or by having the "Escort" skill.
EducationGui_83: Press on the captured ship's icon in the port to repair it, or dismantle them for wood and cloth. Remember, each subsequent repair costs more.
EducationGui_84: Click on the move button and drag the slider to send crew from the reserves to the ship.
EducationGui_86: Click the icon of the ship's weapon to swap it with another from storage.
EducationGui_87: Switch between your ships by clicking the button below.
EducationGui_89: Beware - some attacks are considered crimes that grant black marks! Marks are temporary but apply penalties, including a ban on peaceful flags.
EducationGui_90: Click the encyclopedia icon to learn more about the world and the game. New entries will appear there.
EducationGui_91: Watch out, a storm! Visibility will decrease, and strong winds threaten to #sink the ship. Watch your ship's tilt level.
EducationGui_92: A hidden powder mine just exploded: watch for enemy ships so you don't miss the moment when they deploy a mine.
EducationGui_93: Fire swivel guns [{0}] to deal extra damage or strike retreating ships.
EducationGui_PowderKeg: Deal significant damage by getting close and dropping powder barrels with [{0}]
EducationGui_94: Load heated shot [2] to deal higher close-range damage.
EducationGui_GameTT_EnterHazardZone: Hazardous waters: an area where peaceful flags don't work, and military flagships patrol. Not for fragile ships...
EducationGui_GameTT_FirstBuildingOnPerso: You can #abandon the personal island at any time to claim another one in a different place. Building costs won't be refunded.
EducationGui_GameTT_LegendSkillsWasOpene: Access to the "Legend" skills section unlocked, allowing you to gain extra points.
EducationGui_GameTT_ShipLoadingAndOverlo: The more cargo in the hold, the deeper the ship sits and #slower it moves. Overloading greatly reduces speed and maneuverability.
EducationGui_GameTT_RespawnNotInNearPort: The nearest city wasn’t allied, so you respawned at a different port.
EducationGui_GameTT_UseDoublonesPassingM: Find a settlement on the edge of the map to #upgrade your ship with #doubloons earned in battle. Works only while in mode!
EducationGui_GameTT_FirstCoins: Your in-game account has been credited: you now have {0} coins. You can buy your desired item or bundle using your balance
EducationGui_GameTT_BattleTimer: You cannot enter the port while in combat stance. Each shot fired or damage taken resets the timer!
EducationGui_GameTT_NoCurrentAmmoInCaptu: A captured ship has run out of selected ammunition. #Replenish at sea or in port by clicking its bar and "Hold."
EducationGui_GameTT_CountOfNeedCannons: There are {0} weapons left in the warehouse from the old ship, and you need {1} more to fully equip the new one.
EducationGui_GameTT_LootDivision: Attacking a ship with other players divides the loot, but handling combat together is easier.
EducationGui_GameTT_Whales: Stun whales with cannon fire or exploding barrels, then approach to haul them in. Or use tackle to harpoon them up close!
GameTT_MoveByDoubleClick: Use double-click to quickly transfer full stacks of resources.
EducationGui_GameTT_OpenAuction1: The auction is where #other captains list their goods, cannons, ships. Each port has different lots.
EducationGui_GameTT_OpenAuction2: Scroll through the list to check nearby ports. Selling rare items, like marks, skulls, blueprints, can earn lots of gold.
EducationGui_GameTT_BuyStartPacket: You can speed up your progress by purchasing a starter pack with ready-made ships from the Store
EducationGui_GameTT_SupportProject: Enjoying the project? Support it: even a small purchase in the Store helps the game
EducationGui_GameTT_SupportProject2: As a bonus, your next coin top-up will earn 50% more
gamewiki_crafts_intro: Here are some craft options from the port's workshop, excluding bonuses or upgrades.
gamewiki_cataclysm_text1: Locals say a cataclysm years ago severed ties with the outside world.
gamewiki_cataclysm_text2: Only the Endless Sea remains visible where great nations once stood...
gamewiki_cataclysm_text3: You can go #beyond the #map to explore new territories, but it's highly dangerous.
EducationGui_ss: You're leaving the Archipelago zone. The Endless sea is know for various anomalies and finds, but all items in the hold get lost if you sink.
EducationGui_OpenHoldFromGame: All ships, ammo, weapons, and consumables are available in any port. But each port has its own #resource warehouse.
gamewiki_windandspeed_text1: #Cruise #mode is when the ship gains a speed bonus under full sails. Fast ships gain bonuses quicker, but against the wind, bonuses don't apply.
gamewiki_windandspeed_text2: Use a #spyglass to observe distant activities. Visibility drops at night or in bad weather, and depends on the ship size.
gamewiki_windandspeed_text3: Rivers and canals have #currents that increase speed, even against the wind.
gamewiki_hold_text1: Ammunition and resources have #weight, so choose carefully what to carry.
gamewiki_hold_text2: Ammo and equipment are stored in a #shared #warehouse accessible at any port, but resources are #stored locally, occupying space.
gamewiki_hold_text3: Warehouses for resources have a weight limit. The limit can be increased by upgrading your warehouses, but the amount of upgraded warehouses is limited. You'll have to decide where to build or dismantle them.
gamewiki_trading_text1: Almost all resources are harvested or produced by players: from building materials to food.
gamewiki_trading_text2: Resources can be traded with ports, but Trade House prices depend on #quantity. Only other captains influence quantity. Profit from price differences by transporting between ports.
gamewiki_trading_text3: At the #Auction, captains trade goods, ships, and weapons. Port owners earn a commission from such deals.
gamewiki_trading_text4: Items have unique traits; find efficient ways to trade them instead of selling at the first opportunity.
gamewiki_trading_text5: #Goods are special items transported and sold in regions where they aren't produced, earning higher prices.
gamewiki_flags_text1: Flags define playstyles, activate or remove PvP protection, grant XP and loot bonuses, and allow access to certain ports.
gamewiki_flags_text2: PvP protection does #not #work in the center of the map (inside the red circle) and in the Endless Sea.
gamewiki_flags_text3: While under PvP protection, when multiple players sink a NPS, loot splits proportionally by damage dealt, or players compete for the entire loot.
gamewiki_nps_text1: NPS (Non-Player-Ship) are game-world ships. They can trade, gather resources, do seaway robberies and explore new sea routes much like any other player.
gamewiki_nps_text2: A "red spyglass" over the name means the NPS may attack upon approach.
gamewiki_nps_text3: Many NPS are vital to the economy, despite being targeted for loot. Sinking them can increase your wanted level and attract attacks, even from ships without "red spyglass."
gamewiki_ammonition_text1: Swivel guns fire using a separate button (default [Q]). They lack precise aim, so fire by approximation.
gamewiki_ammonition_text2: Barrels and mines are close-combat tools to evade pursuers but can harm your ship if mishandled.
gamewiki_ammonition_text3: Fireships are boats with powder charges targeting a selected ship. They can be destroyed by enemy fire, even at launch, causing full damage to your ship. So, pick carefully the moment to send them out.
gamewiki_workshop_text1: In the crafting section, you can process raw materials (like ore) into resources, which are then used to create components (such as bulkheads, canvases, plates, bronze) for building ships and upgrades. This requires #craft-#time, which regenerates over real time. Skills affect its limit and respawn rate.
gamewiki_workshop_text2: To craft components, you'll need to unlock a #specialty #workshop. You can have up to two of the Specialty Workshops as you level up your skills and warehouse.
gamewiki_workshop_text3: On your personal island, you can also open a Specialty Workshop that produces items more efficiently than in the port and does not consume regular crafting time (though it does use the island's internal resources).
gamewiki_travallingandhazard_text1: The higher the hazard level of the waters, the more high-rate NPS, better mission rewards, and more loot you can gather from islands and at sea. However, such zones are much more dangerous.
gamewiki_travallingandhazard_text2: There are #shallow waters at sea, restricting access for ships of certain rates. For some, this is a limitation, but for others, it's protection from large ships.
gamewiki_travallingandhazard_text3: You may also encounter reefs that damage ships upon passage. The higher the speed, the greater the damage.
gamewiki_buildingships_text1: Ships are divided into classes. To level up, gain experience with other ships in the same class. The more branches fully upgraded, the greater the experience bonus to other branches.
gamewiki_buildingships_text2: Larger ships are not necessarily better, as they are less maneuverable and more vulnerable.
gamewiki_buildingships_text3: Fast ships are lightly armed, trading ships have large holds, heavy ships have the strongest defense, and combat ships boast the best weapons.
gamewiki_crew_text1: The crew performs many roles: loading cannons, managing sails, and fighting during boarding. The crew members are divided into sailors and boarding parties.
gamewiki_crew_text2: You can hire crew members in any ratio, but a lack of sailors results in #penalties to cannon reload speed and sail handling. Keep in mind that crew members often die during firefights.
gamewiki_crew_text3: Special crew members provide unique benefits to the entire ship. Each has a limited service term and demands extra pay.
gamewiki_crew_text4: These cannot be hired in ports but can be found in #taverns, rescued at sea, obtained from #captives, or recruited on #personal #islands.
gamewiki_crew_text5: When traveling through port taverns, specialties depend on the type: cities feature soldiers and sailors, while pirates are found in pirate coves and pirate-controlled cities. Recruitment chance depends on the ship; higher in special modes. Recruiting on personal islands introduces rarer individuals compared to taverns.
gamewiki_weapons_text1: Weapons are categorized into classes. Cannons are rapid-fire weapons with medium range and damage. Carronades are short-range with high penetration. Culverins have high accuracy and long range but the lowest "damage per minute."
gamewiki_weapons_text2: Reload times always include #volley #time, reducing the effectiveness of rapid-fire weapons on multi-cannon ships.
gamewiki_weapons_text3: Damage equals penetration minus defense. Low #penetration cannons may fail to damage well-protected ships, especially if they face the bow.
gamewiki_weapons_text4: Unneeded cannons can be #smelted in a workshop using coal to obtain iron or copper.
gamewiki_weapons_text5: Cannons can be disabled in battle via #chainshot hits. Repairable only in port or by item. Rarely, cannons #burst (approx. 1 in 100-300 shots), and cannot be restored
gamewiki_powerupitems_text1: Consumables can repair, #enhance ship stats, or provide allies with bonuses.
gamewiki_powerupitems_text2: Defeating NPS or players can reward random consumables in an extra temporary slot.
gamewiki_powerupitems_text3: As with upgrades, creating #combinations with consumables effectively leads to better results.
gamewiki_tradersinsea_text1: At sea, you can find trading ships and various settlements. Their offers are unique and #change daily. These traders sell ammo and provisions and offer profitable deals for buying and selling.
gamewiki_tradersinsea_text2: In the Endless Sea, traders' offers are even more lucrative.
gamewiki_storm_text1: The most dangerous phenomenon is a storm. As you approach the storm's center, you'll face squalls, heavy rain, and large waves. Wind in storm zones grants a #speed #bonus.
gamewiki_storm_text2: Avoid dangerous listing during storms by furling sails on time or using upgrades.
gamewiki_storm_text3: In cold regions, the crew requires additional #provisions to survive, or they will perish. However, here #no #goods spoil, and you can find more resources and fish.
gamewiki_storm_text4: In other regions, hunger levels will never reach critical values, but additional provisions provide a #bonus.
ingamett_foodConsumption: Click the icon of an edible resource in the hold to feed the crew.
gamewiki_factories_text1: In designated areas, you can build your mines and manufactories, that operate without your direct involvement.
gamewiki_factories_text2: The maximum amount of mines and manufactories depends on the skills taken. Constructed buildings can be destroyed.
gamewiki_factories_text3: Typically, at levels 3 and 4 of development, they need to be supplied with provisions. The overall profit does not increase, but they are extremely useful when there is high demand for the produced resource.
gamewiki_boarding_text1: Damage a ship to half and align side-by-side to board. If it flees, shoot Bar Shot at sails
gamewiki_boarding_text2: Monitor the number of enemy crew members. During boarding, #focus fire on enemy units and protect your own crew targeted by the enemy. This can help you "outplay" them.
gamewiki_boarding_text3: Boarding allows you to gain #more #loot - up to the #entire hold and #weapons, whereas sinking the ship results in some resources being lost, even in the Endless Sea.
gamewiki_boarding_text4: You can also replenish your crew, take captives, or #capture the ship (NPS only). During boarding, you might lose some gold; the best way to prevent this is to store savings in chests at ports.
gamewiki_shipscapturing_text1: Successful boarding allows you to capture NPS ships into your fleet. Their main roles are escorting, assisting in battles, and transporting cargo.
gamewiki_shipscapturing_text2: Commands can be given to the fleet via the menu after pressing [Z]. Allied NPS ships can be #repaired and #dismantled in ports (yielding wood and cloth). During arena battles, other modes, or when you're offline, they #wait #in port.
gamewiki_shipscapturing_text3: If your ship sinks or strays too far, NPS ships will lose command and await your return.
Education_Gui_captured_ship: Captured ships will assist in battles. Manage them through the menu [{0}], repair them, and dismantle them in ports.
gamewiki_socialmenu_text1: To interact with another player, click their nickname in-game [Ctrl], in chat, or from the Friends or Guild Members lists.
gamewiki_socialmenu_text2: You can trade with any other players at sea by sailing up to each other and using the social menu. Tradeable resources must be in the hold.
gamewiki_socialmenu_text3: While in a fleet (even with captured ships) or in the arena, you can send quick messages. The default key is [Z].
gamewiki_portscapturing_text1: Controlling ports expands faction territories, generates income, produces resources, and collects taxes.
gamewiki_portscapturing_text2: All military actions require #raising #tension. Once it reaches 100%, #battle #entry becomes available, marked on the map. This occurs only during #battle #windows set by the port owner. At other times, tension caps at 50%.
gamewiki_portscapturing_text3: Each fleet in battle has its own respawn points - outposts. The goal is to destroy all towers and the fort, then land your infantry within the given time. Each destroyed object or ship #extends the #time limit. The maximum fleet size is 20. If the battle ends, your fleet's positions will remain occupied. Make sure to stock up on ammunition in advance, as #ship #integrity in these battles #is #not #lost.
gamewiki_portscapturing_text4: Each fleet has respawn points based on the number of participants. Sinkings reduce the number of points, and when they are depleted, the fleet is removed from the game. Depending on their rate, ships subtract between 2 to 7 points, while heavy ships subtract between 4 to 9.
gamewiki_portscapturing_text5: The guild can use various options for timed #protection, which does not allow for the increase of tension. But, these and any other actions, like repairs, cannot be activated, if there is any tension present.
gamewiki_portscapturing_text6: Capturing ports allows you to control regions, develop cities, and collect taxes and mooring fees. You can only attack ports belonging to enemy factions.
gamewiki_tensity_text1: Raising tension can affect the port's economy and options that the controlling guild has, and can be used as a separate measure of influence
gamewiki_tensity_text2: Starting at 15%, the guild cannot issue decrees, including protection or window changes. At 30%, production efficiency decreases, and beyond that, tax income stops.
gamewiki_tensity_text3: #Tension #rises under faction flags. Multiple guilds can compete for tension, taking points from each other.
gamewiki_tensity_text4: To #decrease #tension, you need to sink pirate NPS under flags, or players that are raising the tension.
gamewiki_tensity_text5: In case of resource shortages, city tension slowly rises up to 100%, after which a #revolt occurs, causing the guild to lose control over the port.
gamewiki_guildsgrow_text1: Influence points are the primary "currency" for development, spent on guild upgrades, captured city enhancements, and increasing member limits.
gamewiki_guildsgrow_text2: The main way to get them is by completing #influence #missions. All members of the guild contribute to the completion of the mission with their actions. The more developed the guild is, the harder the missions will be.
gamewiki_guildsgrow_text3: Influence points earned during a season are displayed in the rankings. Spending points does not decrease your ranking.
gamewiki_portsrobbing_text1: After the port battle becomes available, the attacking guild will be able to rob the fort by destroying it. Compared to capturing it, you do not need to destroy any towers. You will be able to take resources from storage and receive a #construction #license. Warning: when robbing bays, you can only get 1 license.
gamewiki_portsrobbing_text2: Raiding will #decrease your respawn points, and each player who takes resources will leave the port battle. Therefore, it might be better to avoid risks when you have the chance to reclaim the port.
gamewiki_treasury_text1: Every guild has a vault and a treasury
gamewiki_treasury_text2: #The vault consists of chests, which can be voluntarily contributed to by members. It is used to pay for various services, including fortification upgrades, contracts, and investments.
gamewiki_treasury_text3: #The treasury is used for payouts to members and is replenished from external sources. It is managed by those with the proper rights: the leader or first mate, by clicking on a member's nickname (including their own).
gamewiki_treasury_text4: Gold enters the treasury as taxes from captured cities. A part of this gold is immediately paid as salary to members, while the rest is deposited in the corresponding fort. Only after the port battle window is completed is the accumulated gold transported to the guild's treasury. At other times, it can be claimed through capture or plunder. In Arab cities, gold is directly deposited into the treasury.
gamewiki_pvp_text1: Fighting captains at sea is a chance to loot them, earn Battle Marks and experience, complete missions, and enjoy the thrill.
gamewiki_pvp_text2: Reputation affects faction city bonuses and outcomes when sinking players.
gamewiki_wanted_level_text1: Many NPS ships play an important role in the economy, despite being frequently sunk for profit.
gamewiki_wanted_level_text2: Sinking NPS ships in #daylight, especially #traders, increases your notoriety level! It decreases over time.
gamewiki_wanted_level_text3: As your notoriety level rises, ships will #attack you more frequently, and over time, bounty hunters will appear.
gamewiki_ports_text1: Most ports are towns and settlements on Archipelago islands, contested by factions. Cities are more developed, offering guilds the ability to impose mooring fees.
gamewiki_ports_text2: Some ports belong to the neutral Coastal Brotherhood. This organization studies the Cataclysm's causes and effects while helping "newcomers" from beyond the sea adapt.
gamewiki_earning_text1: You can grow your wealth (gold and resources) through these primary methods:
gamewiki_earning_text2: #Gathering: finding and collecting items at sea, exploring islands, and charting the map.
gamewiki_earning_text5: #Capturing and dismantling ships: yielding a large amount of wood and cloth.
gamewiki_earning_text6: #Fishing: use tackle to catch fish, which can be used for sustenance, sold, or traded for items.
gamewiki_earning_text7: #Trading: buy low and sell high. Prices are visible on the map and in trade houses.
gamewiki_earning_text9: #Auction: find profitable deals or sell resources.
gamewiki_earning_text10: #Crafting: create in-demand goods, ships, or cannons, and sell them on the auction.
gamewiki_earning_text11: #Arena: earn Battle Marks that can be sold if surplus.
gamewiki_earning_text12: #Quests and missions: complete combat quests and deliveries from port taverns to gain scrolls and reputation as bonuses.
gamewiki_earning_text13: #Imperial Flagships: rich in resources, blueprints and cannons, which can be melted down into iron if surplus.
gamewiki_earning_text14: #Mines and manufactories produce resources automatically without your involvement.
gamewiki_earning_text15: #Guilds: capture ports, collect taxes and mooring fees, and produce resources.
gamewiki_earning_text16: There are always high-demand goods in the economy. It's most effective to focus on them. When planning routes, aim for multiple goals: trade, complete missions, collect items, and plan the transport of your goods efficiently.

fraction_Espaniol_Desc: The Viceroyalty of Espaniol was founded by descendants of Spanish, French, and Dutch colonists. Its head of state is Viceroy Alfonso III de Castro. ##Under his command, you will expand faction territory, develop cities, complete missions to earn influence, and contribute to seasonal victory. ##Allied cities grant various economic and combat bonuses. ##All members must maintain friendly reputation or be expelled
fraction_Espaniol_Desc_Short: A state founded by descendants of Spanish, French, and Dutch colonists.
fraction_Antilia_Desc: The British Dominion of Antilia was founded by descendants of English and German colonists. Current ruler: Lord Protector John Campbell. ##Under him, you will expand territory, develop cities, complete missions to earn influence, and contribute to seasonal victory. ##Allied cities offer combat and economic bonuses. ##Members must maintain friendly rep or be expelled
fraction_Antilia_Desc_Short: A state founded by descendants of English and German colonists.
fraction_KaiAndSeveria_Desc: A dynastic union between Princess Lau Mi of the Kingdom of Kai and Afanasy Galitsyn, governor of the Severian Republic. ##Expand faction territory, grow cities, complete missions, earn influence, and push for seasonal victory. ##Allied cities provide economic and combat bonuses. ##All members must keep friendly reputation or be expelled
fraction_KaiAndSeveria_Desc_Short: Formed through the marriage of Princess Kai - Lau Mi and Athanasius Galitsyn, governor of the Severian Merchant Union
fraction_Pirate_Desc: Pirates are the only faction with no rules or restrictions. Pirate guilds may be enemies or allies. ##You can plunder cities, attack the Trade Union, and claim pirate coves under special conditions. ##◈ Peace flags unavailable - only false flags. #◈ Can't use other factions' auction houses. #◈ Always hostile with military factions. #◈ Trusted guilds can join you in PB only during attack ##Earn pirate tokens for all sinkings. It's tough, but exciting!
fraction_Pirate_Desc_Short: Pirates are the only faction without rules or restrictions. Pirate guilds within the faction can be either enemies or allies.
fraction_TradeUnion_Desc: The Trade Union (Khanfar Caliphate) is a neutral faction that cannot capture territory but builds economic hubs. Nations pledge not to attack it. ##In exchange, it pays a 1/200 cargo safety fee in every city except pirate ones. ##Members must keep neutral or positive rep with Archipelago states or be expelled. ##Perfect for peaceful play. The Trade Union supplies warring cities
fraction_TradeUnion_Desc_Short: The Trade Union (Khanfar Caliphate) is a trade faction that can't capture cities, and is recognized as neutral by the states
fraction_Empire_Desc: A state from the World of Sea Peoples. The metropolis is located on the Nameless Continent, far to the southwest of the Archipelago, beyond the Endless Sea. The Empire seeks to conquer the Archipelago and free it from colonizers
flotilia_no_contract: A flotilla is not tied to any faction and uses member reputations. ##Great for small groups or training, with a simple hierarchy. ##Later, upgrade to a guild and pick a faction
GuildsWindow_createText_flotilia: A flotilla is a simplified guild with similar principles but fewer available options
GuildsWindow_createText_guild: A guild is a large organization formed for a common purpose or interest, featuring a hierarchy, participating in global events and trade, forming alliances, and declaring wars

gamewiki_guildsfractions_text1: A guild is a large organization within a faction with its own hierarchy
gamewiki_guildsfractions_fraction_1_text: After the Cataclysm and the Age of Anarchy, former European colonies became independent states, competing for control over the Archipelago.
All states in the Archipelago are at #war with each other over territories and cities
gamewiki_guildsfractions_fraction_2_text: Due to a crisis of authority, many sailors formed small groups to take control of their own fates, rejecting laws. Political exiles, deserters, and notorious criminals are often becoming pirates as well.
gamewiki_guildsfractions_fraction_3_text: The Trade Union represents trade colonies of the enigmatic Khanfar Caliphate, located beyond the Archipelago. It resembles Arabic cultures. This neutral faction cannot capture territories.
gamewiki_guildsfractions_fraction_4_text: The Empire (officially: Inhagger Empire) hails from the Sea Peoples' World. The metropolis lies on the Nameless Continent, southwest of the Archipelago, beyond the Endless Sea. It seeks to conquer the Archipelago. None of the Archipelago's states have established diplomatic ties with it.
It has missions in the Endless Sea, serving as supply bases for raids and major attacks on the Archipelago.
Rumors say imperial navigators can cross the "eternal storm" in the Endless Sea, and acquiring ships crafted by imperial shipwrights is the dream of many captains.
gamewiki_hqs_text1: As a city develops, it grows larger and provides #more #bonuses to its owner and allied guilds. For military factions, allied guilds include all guilds, but for pirates, only trusted ones are included.
gamewiki_hqs_text2: A trading guild can open #trade #missions to receive bonuses by paying a fee, which depends on the city's level. It must pay regularly to maintain the mission.
gamewiki_hqs_text3: Allied guilds must make mandatory contributions to receive bonuses. This encourages factions to develop cities together.

ship_72_desc: This frigate served the fleet for over 55 years, taking part both in naval operations and in delicate diplomatic missions, including transporting members of the royal family. The Southampton became the prototype for the 32-gun frigates of the same name, which played an important role in the British fleet in the late 18th century.
ship_73_desc: One of the earliest ships of the Española school of shipbuilding. This frigate class was first laid down at the San Martinas shipyard. Widely employed for patrol duty, protecting trade routes from pirates, and conducting raids deep into enemy waters.
ship_74_desc: This ship belonged to the large and successful "Slava Rossii" class of ships of the line (built from 1733 to 1774 in St. Petersburg and Arkhangelsk; 58 vessels in total), known for their seaworthiness, maneuverability, and stability. "Three Hierarchs" was one of the key ships of the first major ocean expedition of the Russian fleet from the Baltic to the Mediterranean, and a symbol of the Chesme victory.
ship_75_desc: This ship was funded by the people of Cantabria ("the mountain" region of Spain, hence the name Montañés - "the Highlander"). It became the benchmark for later Spanish 74-gun ships of the line of the late 18th century. It took part in almost every major naval engagement from the late 18th to the early 19th century.

IngamePollWindow_support_us_1: The game is created by a small team of enthusiasts who love games and the Age of Sail. We have no publishers, and any support means a lot to us.
IngamePollWindow_support_us_2: Please leave a review to help us grow:
interest_point_1_desc: The islands were discovered by Spanish and Portuguese explorers of the pre-Columbian era. The first Old World settlements appeared here in the 15th century. Shallow bays could not host large ships, which partly pushed Europeans deeper into the Archipelago.
interest_point_2_desc: Currents from the Endless Sea bring huge fish schools here, making these waters vital for the island fishing communities.
interest_point_3_name: Islas Díaz Ahogado (Isles of the Drowned God)
interest_point_3_desc: After the Great Cataclysm, the Sea God lived on these islands. He chose to live among humans and became a renegade. With his aid, humans won a crucial victory over the Empire and sealed the southern portal to the Endless Sea, weakening the Imperial advance. In that battle, the renegade God’s flagship hit mines and sank into the depths. Since then he has been called the Drowned God, protector of all souls lost at sea.
interest_point_4_desc: In 1492, on one of these islands, Christopher Columbus founded a fortified settlement built from the remains of the Spanish ship Santa María. He named it La Navidad ("Christmas") in memory of the shipwreck on 25 December. It became the first European colony in the Archipelago. In the 16th century, the town rose to become the capital of all Spanish lands in the New World, even hosting the king's residence. But once Spain seized Sabatin, Espaniol, and Yucatan, the minor island colonies, La Navidad among them, lost their former importance. By the mid-16th century, San Martinas on Sabatin had taken La Navidad’s place, favored for its stronger harbor and better port defenses.
interest_point_5_desc: The inland sea of the Espaniolan Kingdom. A vital route linking its ports and the foundation of conquistador power.
interest_point_6_desc: Discovered by Christopher Columbus on 10 May 1503 during his fourth voyage to the New World. After the Cataclysm, Spain’s power crumbled, and the islands fell under Stevenson; once an English officer, later a pirate. Centuries passed, Columbus’s name for the islands faded, and they came to bear the name of the legendary corsair.
interest_point_7_name: Strait of a Thousand Dead Man
interest_point_7_desc: A sea graveyard of hundreds of ships. Raging currents, whirlpools, and sudden icy winds could freeze a ship’s crew within minutes. But those days seem gone, and navigation is once again possible. Yet these waters still inspire fear. What if the cold returns tomorrow?
interest_point_8_desc: Once home to a local tribe of the same name. During the Cataclysm, northern frost wiped them out. They vanished over a century ago, and only the islands’ name recalls their existence.
interest_point_9_desc: The name of a volcano rising above waters of staggering depth.Though located in the Archipelago’s center, this route is avoided due to unpredictable gas bubbles that can poison a ship’s crew.
interest_point_10_desc: Many battles took place here between Espaniol, the Empire, and the Sea Peoples. Hundreds of ships rest in the silent deep. A series of crushing defeats shattered Espaniola’s dominance in the Archipelago.
interest_point_11_desc: Once lands of a powerful Indian kingdom, later conquered by the Kaitans. The locals perished or were absorbed by colonizers.
interest_point_12_desc: Long uninhabited, the islands began to be settled only after the Archipelago’s warming.
interest_point_13_desc: These waters were frozen for ages, making navigation impossible. After the warming and the founding of Helbion, the region revived. Local northern fish species are highly valued and attract many fishermen.
interest_point_14_desc: Named after Nozul, one of the six "immortal" admirals and the Imperial Lord, who ruled these waters for over fifty years. Lord Nozul was claimed by the depths with his black ship in the Battle of Lagonaf.
interest_point_15_desc: Local legends say dragons once nested on these islands. People stole dragon eggs to raise and ride them. Only one in ten dragon hatchlings survived captivity. But tame dragons never bred, and eventually went extinct.
interest_point_16_desc: Domain of the Khanfar Caliphate, whose cities are said to have appeared overnight on once-empty islands. Trade thrives here and the Bay’s prosperous cities offer exotic goods. The Caliphate does not seek new conquests in the Archipelago. Yet many sense a dark secret behind this mirage made real.
cball_ny_desc1: Round shot made of packed snow. For unknown reasons, it deals damage only to the Sea Elves - strange vessels that entered the Archipelago’s waters on Christmas Eve.
